definition.en: Imagination is a property of mind, not brain, and no physical tool can force the imagination to conceive of an original conception or idea.
definition.zh: 想象力是心灵的属性，而非大脑的属性，没有任何物理工具能迫使想象力构想出原创的概念或想法。

definition.en: The imagination is in reality a connective fiber between the physical individual and the nonphysical entity.
definition.zh: 想象力实际上是物质个体与非物质存有之间的连接纤维。

definition.en: The imagination belongs to the language of the psyche.
definition.zh: 想象力属于心灵的语言。

definition.en: The imagination is highly involved with event-forming.
definition.zh: 想象力高度参与事件的成形。
想象力基本上从不具有破坏性。

explanation:
想象力是清醒状态下人与梦境宇宙的连接通道。它重新表述梦境数据，并将其应用于现实世界的具体情境或问题。想象力本质上从不具有破坏性，它属于心智而非大脑的属性，因此任何物理工具都无法强迫它产生原创概念。

想象力通过将信念转化为物理体验来塑造你的现实世界。它赋予信念流动性，是推动信念转化为实际经验的核心动力。你体验的世界、你与他人建立的关系，都深受你想象力的影响——你往往是对自己投射出的"想象形象"做出反应，而非对真实的他人。因此，必须学会用想象力将概念在脑海中移动，以替换不适合的信念。

想象力处理的是尚未物理显现的广阔现实领域，而理性通常处理眼前世界的证据。但任何想象行为都包含理性，

quotes.text: Now, and this can hardly be called a controversial statement, the imagination is waking mans connection with the universe of dreams. Imagination often restates dream data, and applies it to particular circumstances or problems within the physical universe. Imagination is never basically destructive. In some cases the ego construction may be weak, or incapable of holding its own, in which case the imagination is often said to be too excitable, and to be at fault.
quotes.translation: 现在，这几乎算不上一个有争议的说法：想象力是清醒之人与梦境宇宙的连接。想象力常常重新陈述梦的数据，并将其应用于物质宇宙中的特定情境或问题。想象力本质上从不是破坏性的。在某些情况下，自我结构可能薄弱，或无法维持自身，此时人们常说想象力过于兴奋，并归咎于它。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The brain is of your plane. You may say that the brain is the mind in camouflage. Imagination belongs to the mind. It can be used by and is used by the brain for purposes of survival, and can sometimes be probed by physical instruments. That is physical instruments can be made to make the imagination move on occasion. But imagination is a property of mind, not brain, and no physical tool can force the imagination to conceive of an original conception or idea.
quotes.translation: 大脑属于你的层面。你可以说大脑是伪装中的心智。想象力属于心智。它可以被大脑用于生存目的，有时也能被物理仪器探测。也就是说，物理仪器可以被制造出来，偶尔促使想象力运作。但想象力是心智的属性，而非大脑的属性，没有任何物理工具能迫使想象力构想出原创的概念或想法。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: I realize that when you discuss him that you say--I know he has failings. This is to assure yourself that, after all, the male is not so all powerful. But you do not see this man's good points or failings clearly. Some of the qualities that you imagine in him as virtues are not and some of the qualities that you imagine to be failings are not failings. You will never have any relationship with the Dick Reed that you have projected upon a living human being. You may have a relationship with that human being, but there is a world of difference between that human being and the imagined image of him to which you react. And it is that image that you see when you look at him and when think of him. That imagined image is real in your mind, it is reality. But you cannot project that image upon another human being and deny him his own reality. You have no chance in a thousand lives of having a relationship with the man you think of as being Dick Reed, because you cannot have a two-way relationship with an image that is one-sided and has no flesh. Now give us a moment. While we are beginning a job, we may as well do a good one.
quotes.translation: 我意识到，当你讨论他时，你会说——我知道他有缺点。这是为了向自己保证，毕竟男性并非如此全能。但你并未清晰地看到这个人的优点或缺点。你想象中他的一些美德并非美德，你想象中他的一些缺点也并非缺点。你永远无法与你投射到活生生的人身上的那个迪克·里德建立任何关系。你可以与那个人建立关系，但那个人与你所反应的那个想象形象之间有天壤之别。当你看着他、想着他时，你看到的就是那个形象。那个想象形象在你的心智中是真实的，它是实相。但你不能将那个形象投射到另一个人身上，并否认他自身的实相。在一千次生命中，你都没有机会与你认为是迪克·里德的那个人建立关系，因为你无法与一个片面、没有血肉的形象建立双向关系。现在给我们一点时间。既然我们开始了一项工作，我们不妨把它做好。

quotes.text: Again, man directs his existence through the use of his imagination -- a feat that does distinguish him from the animals. What connects people and separates them is the power of idea and the force of imagination. Patriotism, family loyalty, political affiliations -- the ideas behind these have the greatest practical applications in your world. You project yourselves into time like children through freely imagining your growth. You instantly color physical experience and nature itself with the tints of your unique imaginative processes. Unless you think quite consistently -- and deeply -- the importance of the imagination quite escapes you, and yet it literally forms the world that you experience and the mass world in which you live.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，人通过运用想象力来引导自己的存在——这一壮举确实使他与动物区别开来。连接人们并使他们分离的是思想的力量和想象力的力量。爱国主义、家庭忠诚、政治归属——这些背后的思想在你们的世界中具有最实际的应用。你们像孩子一样，通过自由想象自己的成长，将自己投射到时间中。你们立即用独特想象过程的色调为物理体验和自然本身着色。除非你相当一致且深入地思考，否则想象力的重要性会完全被你忽略，然而它实际上形成了你所体验的世界以及你生活的群体世界。

quotes.text: In recent times the trend has been in the opposite direction, so that the abilities of the imagination were considered highly suspect, while exterior events were considered the only aspects of reality. You ended up with a true-or-false kind of world, in which it seemed that the answers to the deepest questions about life could be answered quite correctly and adequately by some multiple-choice test. Man's imagination seemed then to be allied with falsehood, unless its products could be turned to advantage in the materialistic existence. In that context, the imagination was tolerated at all only because it sometimes offered new technological inventions.
quotes.translation: 近代以来，趋势走向了相反的方向，以至于想象力的能力被高度怀疑，而外在事件被视为实相的唯一方面。你最终陷入了一个非真即假的世界，在那里，关于生命最深问题的答案似乎可以通过某种多项选择测试得到相当正确和充分的回答。人的想象力似乎与虚假结盟，除非其产物能在物质主义存在中转化为优势。在这种背景下，想象力之所以被容忍，仅仅是因为它有时提供了新的技术发明。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The imagination has always dealt with creativity, and as man began to settle upon a kind of consciousness that dealt with cause and effect, he no longer physically perceived the products of his imagination directly in the old manner. He realized in those earlier times that illness, for instance, was initially as much the result of the imagination as health was, for he experienced far more directly the brilliant character of his own imagination. The lines between imaginative and physical experience have blurred for you, and of course they have also become tempered by other beliefs and the experiences that those beliefs then engender.
quotes.translation: 想象力始终与创造力打交道，当人开始定居于一种处理因果关系的意识时，他不再以旧方式直接物理感知自己想象力的产物。在那些早期时代，他意识到，例如，疾病最初与健康一样，同样是想象力的结果，因为他更直接地体验到自己想象力的辉煌特质。对你们而言，想象力体验与物理体验之间的界限已经模糊，当然，它们也已被其他信念以及这些信念随后产生的体验所调和。

quotes.text: Give us a moment, then ... Imagination also plays an important part in your subjective life, as it gives mobility to your beliefs. It is one of the motivating agencies that helps transform your beliefs into physical experience. It is vital therefore that you understand the interrelationship between ideas and imagination. In order to dislodge unsuitable beliefs and establish new ones, you must learn to use your imagination to move concepts in and out of your mind. The proper use of imagination can then propel ideas in the directions you desire.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……想象力在你的主观生活中也扮演着重要角色，因为它赋予你的信念以流动性。它是帮助你将信念转化为物质体验的驱动媒介之一。因此，理解观念与想象力之间的相互关系至关重要。为了摒弃不合适的信念并建立新的信念，你必须学会运用想象力将概念在脑海中移入移出。正确运用想象力便能将观念推向你所期望的方向。

quotes.text: This is certainly contradictory. Fantasy left you cold. I say this icily. Intellectually you would have no part in it, yet your imagination spun its fantastic web despite the fact that you believed in neither fantasy or web. Your artwork showed promise from the beginning. Your imagination was rich and varied. But you so feared your present mother's sense of exaggeration, that led often to sheer though unwitting lies and dishonesty, that you denied the capacity of the imagination lest it also lead you into ways of deceit.
quotes.translation: 这显然自相矛盾。幻想让你无动于衷。我冷冷地说出这句话。理智上你拒绝参与其中，然而你的想象力却编织出奇妙的网——尽管你既不相信幻想也不相信这张网。你的艺术作品从一开始就展现出潜力。你的想象力丰富而多元。但你如此恐惧你现世母亲那种夸张的倾向（这常导致纯粹但无心的谎言与不诚实），以至于你否定了想象力的能力，唯恐它也会将你引向欺骗之路。

quotes.text: In your mind's eye however imagine a run-down, shabby, deteriorating shamble of a house with rotting floorboards and sagging porches. Then imagine that it is burned to the ground and the remaining rubble carted away and burned. Imagine the land now free beneath it, open to the blessing of wind, rain and sun. Then imagine a new house being built there, of your preferred choice, with all new materials, of splendid design, and see this always in your mind where before you saw the previous image.
quotes.translation: 然而，请在你心灵之眼中想象一座破败、衰朽、摇摇欲坠的房屋，地板腐烂，门廊塌陷。接着想象它被烧成灰烬，残余的瓦砾被运走焚毁。想象那片土地如今重获自由，沐浴在风、雨和阳光的祝福中。然后想象在那里建起一栋你心仪的新屋，全部采用崭新材料，设计精妙绝伦——在你曾看到旧影像的地方，始终在脑海中观想这幅画面。

quotes.text: If the exercise is done correctly it is literally impossible for the old idea to obtain any energy for its continuance and your attention is directed to the desired end. The object should be something you can visualize easily however. If you have difficulty imagining the deliberate destruction of the negative object, this is merely a sign of its hold. You may then instead imagine its destruction by an act of nature. The house being struck by lightning for example. If this is the case then the exercise should be continued until you imagine you yourself deciding upon and bringing about the destruction and replacement. If you are not ready to burn down the structure itself, imaginatively, then you are not prepared to rid yourself of the negative behavior, you see. The symbolic destruction is the real destruction. The symbolic creation is the real creation. Such exercises will cut down the physical time involved however.
quotes.translation: 若正确执行此练习，旧观念根本不可能获得任何能量来延续，你的注意力将完全导向渴望达成的结果。不过，目标物应选择你能轻易视觉化的对象。若你难以想象刻意摧毁负面对象，这恰恰表明其掌控力之强。此时你可转而想象它被自然之力摧毁——例如房屋遭雷击。若属此情形，则需持续练习，直至你能想象自己主动决定并实施摧毁与重建。若你尚未准备好（在想象中）焚毁那栋建筑，便意味着你尚未准备好摆脱负面行为，明白吗？象征性的摧毁即是真实的摧毁，象征性的创造即是真实的创造。这类练习将缩短物质时间。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: I do not mean to idealize him either, or others of his kind, but to point out that you can use your imaginations and intellects in other fashions than you do. In fact, such fashions are not only genetically possible, but genetically probable--a matter I will discuss later in the book. The imagination, of course, deals with the implied universe, those vast areas of reality that are not physically manifest, while reason usually deals with the evidence of the world that is before it. That statement is generally true, but specifically, of course, any act of the imagination involves reasoning, and any [act] of reason involves the imagination.
quotes.translation: 我并非要将他或同类人理想化，而是指出你可以用不同于现在的方式运用想象力与理智。事实上，这类方式不仅在基因层面可能，更在基因层面具有或然性——这是本书后续将探讨的议题。想象力当然涉及隐含的宇宙，那些未在物质层面显化的广阔实相领域；而理性通常处理眼前世界的证据。此陈述大体正确，但具体而言，任何想象力行为都包含理性，任何理性行为也都包含想象力。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Emotion and imagination are being considered as far superior. The displaced powers of consciousness are still being assigned to the unconscious, and great efforts are being made to reach what seem to be normally inaccessible areas of awareness. To this end drugs are utilized, cults set up, and there are methods and training manuals galore. Period. Yet there is nothing basically inaccessible about such "inner knowledge or experience." It can all be quite conscious, and utilized to enrich the reality that you know. The conscious mind is not some prodigal child or poor relative of the self. It can quite freely focus into inner reality when you understand that it can. You, again, have a conscious mind. You can change the focus of your own consciousness.
quotes.translation: 情感与想象力正被视作远为优越的存在。意识中被置换的力量仍被归入无意识领域，人们正竭力触及那些看似通常无法企及的觉知领域。为此，药物被使用，教派被建立，方法手册与训练指南层出不穷。句号。然而，这类"内在知识或体验"本质上并无不可企及之处。它们完全可以被清醒觉知，并用于丰富你所知的实相。意识心并非自我的败家子或穷亲戚。当你理解它能够聚焦内在实相时，它便能自由地做到这一点。再次强调，你拥有意识心。你可以改变自身意识的焦点。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Imagine if you can the figures or inhabitants in the painting having psychological reality, all within the set limits prescribed by the given space. Imagine in other words consciousness, growth, reality and expansion, having nothing to do with expansion of space in your terms, but an almost complete freedom of psychological realities, and you will come at least within the realm of understanding what I mean by an expanding universe that has nothing to do with the expanding universe of which your scientists speak.
quotes.translation: 请想象画中的人物或居民具有心理实相，全都存在于特定空间所设定的界限内。换句话说，想象意识、成长、实相与扩张，与你们所谓的空间扩张毫无关系，却拥有心理实相的几乎完全自由——这样你至少能开始理解我所指的"扩张宇宙"：它完全不同于科学家们谈论的那种宇宙膨胀。

quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Alter your time orientation in other such exercises. This will automatically allow you to break away from too narrow a focus. It will to some extent break apart the rigid interlocking of your perception into reality as you have learned how to perceive it. Children can play so vividly that they might, for example, imagine themselves parched under a desert sun, though they are in the middle of the coolest air-conditioned living room. They are on the one hand completely involved in their activity, yet on the other hand they are quite aware of their "normal" environment. Yet the adult often fears that any such playful unofficial alteration of consciousness is dangerous, and becomes worried that the imagined situation will supersede the real one.
quotes.translation: 在其他类似练习中改变你的时间取向。这会自动让你摆脱过于狭隘的焦点，并在某种程度上打破你习以为常的感知实相之僵固连锁。孩子们可以玩得如此生动逼真，比如尽管身处最凉爽的空调客厅，他们却能想象自己在沙漠烈日下焦渴难耐。他们一方面完全投入活动，另一方面又清楚知道"正常"环境。然而成年人常害怕任何这种嬉戏式的非正式意识改变是危险的，担心想象情境会取代真实情境。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There are five steps. (Pause.) It is possible to cover all of them at once, or the process may take you some time. First however imagine your consciousness out of your body in its astral form. Next, feeling that you are in the astral form, tell yourself that from that image you will be able to see the image of the person that you were in your immediately past life. I cannot tell you whether you will succeed at once, as you may, or whether you will find it difficult. You, Joseph, being proficient with images, may find it easier than Ruburt. Now when you feel that you have this new image of your past self before you, then imagine that your consciousness is moving from your astral form into this past self. You should not do this until you see the whole form.
quotes.translation: 共有五个步骤。（停顿。）可能一次性全部完成，也可能需要一些时间。首先，想象你的意识以星光体形式离开身体。接着，在感觉自己处于星光体状态时，告诉自己从这个影像中你将能看到你上一世那个人的影像。我无法告诉你是否会立即成功——可能立刻成功，也可能发现很困难。约瑟，你擅长处理影像，可能比鲁柏更容易做到。当你感觉这个前世自我的新影像出现在面前时，再想象你的意识从星光体移入这个前世自我。在看清完整形体之前不应进行这一步。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The imagination is highly involved with event-forming. Children's imaginations prevent them from being too limited by their parents' world. Waking or dreaming, children "pretend." In their pretending they exercise their consciousness in a particularly advantageous way. While accepting a given reality for themselves, they nevertheless reserve the right, so to speak, to experiment with other "secondary" states of being. To some extent they become what they are pretending to be, and in so doing they also increase their own knowledge and experience. Left alone, children would learn how to cope with animals by pretending to be animals, for example. Through experiencing the animals' reactions, they would understand how to react themselves.
quotes.translation: 想象力与事件形成高度相关。儿童的想象力使他们免于被父母的世界过度限制。无论清醒或做梦，孩子们都在"假装"。在假装中，他们以特别有利的方式锻炼意识。在为自己接受既定实相的同时，他们仍保留尝试其他"次级"存在状态的权利。某种程度上，他们变成了自己假装的对象，并因此增长自身知识与经验。例如，若不加干预，孩子们会通过假装成动物来学习如何与动物相处——通过体验动物的反应，他们就能理解自己该如何反应。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: But never as a finished product. For a state of health is not an end product.... or an unchanging station in those terms. It is the ability to effectively handle energy in a constructive way for your own benefit and the benefit of others. The state of health is a poor term. You should indeed imagine yourself, therefore, able to handle your energy effectively for your own good and the good of others; to imagine yourself as a channel through which the creativity of the universe can express itself. For when you harbor negative ideas and resentments, then indeed you set up a block and the block causes distortions. Now you call them illnesses in many instances. They are distortions. The energy is being distorted and misused and misshapen.

quotes.text: Here is an analogy. Imagine then the inner vitality being some cosmic sphere, but a sphere of more dimensions than you can imagine. quotes.text: All of the probabilities practically possible in human development are therefore present to some extent or another in each individual. Any biological or spiritual advancement that you might imagine will of course not come from any outside agency, but from within the heritage of consciousness made flesh. Generally, those alive in this century chose a particular kind of orientation. The species chose to specialize in certain kinds of physical manipulation, to devote its energies in certain directions. Those directions have brought forth a reality unique in its own fashion. Man has not driven himself down a blind alley, in other words. He has been studying the nature of his consciousness -- using it as if it were apart from the rest of nature, and therefore seeing nature and the world in a particular light.6 That light has finally made him feel isolated, alone, and to some extent relatively powerless (intently).
quotes.translation: 因此，人类发展中所有实际可能的概率，都在每个人身上以某种程度呈现。你们所能想象的任何生物或灵性进步，当然不会来自任何外在力量，而是源于意识化为血肉的内在传承。一般而言，活在这个世纪的人选择了某种特定的取向。这个物种选择专注于某些物理操控方式，将能量投入特定方向。这些方向产生了一种独特风格的实相。换句话说，人类并非把自己逼入了死胡同。他一直在研究自己意识的本质——仿佛将意识视为与自然其余部分分离之物，因此以特定视角看待自然与世界。这种视角最终让他感到孤立、孤独，并在某种程度上相对无力（专注地）。

quotes.text: This is simplified. Consciously you deal with those problems of one given physical life. Consciously you are not aware of what you call reincarnations, though these occur within your own system. Now these are not separate existences at all. They only appear so to you. Your earth identity (smile) in singular terms, is one existence. When you sleep at night and awaken, you do not suppose yourself a new personality, or imagine that you have died during the night. This is what you do when you imagine that you have several existences as different people. Your real existence, even within your system, is too much for you to deal with in conscious terms, therefore the seeming segments. You know you live these seemingly different lives simultaneously, as you gain development, but it is always you gaining development in a variety of roles. I stress therefore that unity. It is only because consciously you do not realize the unity that the various reincarnations seem so diverse and separate.
quotes.translation: 这是简化的说法。有意识地，你处理的是某一特定物质生活的问题。有意识地，你并不知道你所谓的转世，尽管这些发生在你自己的系统内。现在，这些根本不是独立的存在。它们只是对你来说如此。你的地球身份（微笑）以单数形式来说，是一个存在。当你晚上睡觉并醒来时，你不会认为自己是一个新的人格，或想象自己已经在夜间死亡。这就是当你想象自己作为不同的人拥有多个存在时所做的事情。你真实的存在，即使在你自己的系统内，也太多以至于你无法有意识地处理，因此才有了这些看似片段的部分。你知道你同时过着这些看似不同的生活，随着你获得发展，但始终是你在各种角色中获得发展。因此我强调那个统一性。仅仅是因为有意识地你没有意识到这种统一性，各种转世才显得如此多样和分离。

quotes.text: The more intense your imagination and inner experience, therefore, the more important it is that you realize the methods by which this inner experience becomes physically real. Your thoughts and emotions begin their journey into physical actualization at the moment of conception. If you happen to live in an area where the coordinate environment is strong, one of those areas I have spoken of as unusually conducive, then it will seem that you are deluged by illnesses or disasters, if these are the nature of your thoughts, because all thought is so fertile in this environment. If, on the other hand, your feelings and subjective experience are fairly well balanced, fairly optimistic and creative in a constructive manner, then it will seem to you that you have been blessed with unusual luck, for your pleasant suppositions will come to pass so quickly.
quotes.translation: 因此，你的想象力和内在体验越强烈，你就越需要认识到这些内在体验变成物理现实的方法。你的思想和情绪在受孕的那一刻就开始了它们进入物理实现的旅程。如果你碰巧生活在一个坐标环境强烈的区域，即我所说的那些异常有利的区域之一，那么如果你的思想本质如此，似乎你会被疾病或灾难淹没，因为在这个环境中所有思想都如此丰饶。另一方面，如果你的感受和主观体验相当平衡，相当乐观并以建设性的方式具有创造性，那么对你来说，似乎你被赋予了不寻常的好运，因为你愉快的假设会如此迅速地实现。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You may feel that you want to "relive" certain episodes of your life so that you can understand them better. Your life's experience, therefore, is your own. Such conditions certainly are not alien. In ordinary living, you often imagine yourself behaving in a different manner than you did, or in your mind reexperiencing events in order to gain greater understanding from them. Your life is your own personal experience- perspective, and when at death you take it out of the mass physical time context, then you can experience it in many ways. Events and objects are not absolute, remember, but plastic. Events can be changed both before and after their occurrence. They are never stable or permanent, even though within the context of three-dimensional reality they may appear so.
quotes.translation: 你或许想要“重温”生命中的某些片段，以便更深入地理解它们。因此，你的人生经历是你自己的。这样的状况绝非陌生。在日常生活中，你常会想象自己以不同于以往的方式行事，或在心中重新体验事件，以求从中获得更深的理解。你的生命是你个人的经验视角，当死亡之际你将其从集体物理时间脉络中抽离，便能以多种方式体验它。记住，事件与物体并非绝对，而是可塑的。事件在发生前后皆可改变。它们从未稳定或恒久，即便在三维实相的脉络中它们看似如此。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Forget now that in this case such a division occurred, and imagine instead the successive thoughts and feelings that you possess. When you feel weak you are weak. When you feel joyful your body benefits and becomes stronger. Augustus's case simply shows in exaggerated form the effects of your beliefs upon your physical image. If you think, "Aha, then from now on I will only think good thoughts -- and therefore be healthy, and inhibit my 'bad' thoughts, or do anything at all with them but think them," then in your own way you are doing what Augustus did. He began by believing that some of his thoughts were so evil that they must somehow be made nonexistent. So inhibiting what you consider as negative thoughts, or assuming that they are so terrible, is no answer.
quotes.translation: 暂且忘记在此案例中发生了这样的分裂，转而想象你所拥有的连续思绪与感受。当你感到虚弱时，你便是虚弱的。当你感到喜悦时，你的身体受益并变得更强壮。奥古斯都的案例只是以夸张的形式展现了你的信念对你身体形象的影响。如果你心想：“啊哈，那么从今以后我只想好的念头——因此就会健康，并抑制我的‘坏’念头，或对它们做任何事，但就是不去想它们，”那么你就在以自己的方式做着奥古斯都所做的事。他始于相信他的一些念头如此邪恶，以至于必须以某种方式让它们不存在。因此，抑制你认为的负面念头，或假设它们如此可怕，并非解决之道。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You must look within yourself for evidences of what you want in terms of positive experience. Examine your past with that in mind. Imagine your future from the power point of the present. In such a way at least you are not using the past to reinforce your limitations, or projecting them into the future. It is only natural to contrast what you want with what you have, and it is very easy to become discouraged in so doing, but looking for errors in the past will not help you. A correctly utilized five-minute period of time can be of great benefit, however. In this period concentrate upon the fact that the point of power is now. Feel and dwell upon the certainty that your emotional, spiritual and psychic abilities are focused through the flesh, and for five minutes only direct all of your attention toward what you want. Use visualization or verbal thought -- whatever comes most naturally to you; but for that period do not concentrate upon any lacks, just upon your desire.
quotes.translation: 你必须在内心寻找你想要的正面经验的证据。带着这个意图审视你的过去。从当下的力量点想象你的未来。至少这样，你不会用过去来强化你的局限，或将它们投射到未来。将你想要的与你所拥有的进行对比是再自然不过的事，而这样做时很容易感到气馁，但在过去寻找错误对你毫无帮助。然而，正确利用的五分钟时间却能带来巨大益处。在这五分钟里，专注于“力量点就在当下”这个事实。感受并沉浸于这样的确信：你的情感、灵性与心灵能力都透过肉身聚焦，并且只在这五分钟内，将你所有的注意力导向你所渴望的。运用视觉化或言语思维——任何对你最自然的方式；但在这段时间内，不要专注于任何匮乏，只专注于你的渴望。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

related_concepts.name_en: The point of power is in the present

quotes.text: Beyond this there are experiences but seldom recalled, in which the usual identification of your consciousness with physical-life orientation is gone. (Pause.) Images as you think of them are based upon your own neurological structure, and your interpretations of these. When you consider survival after death, for instance, you imagine all the senses fully operating, though perhaps in a nonphysical body. Perception without images seems impossible in that context. Yet in some dream situations you enter a state of awareness quite divorced from that kind of sense data. Images as such are not involved, though later they may be manufactured unconsciously for the sake of translation. In those conditions you come close to an understanding of what your consciousness is when it is not physically oriented at all.
quotes.translation: 除此之外，还有一些鲜少被忆起的经验，在其中你意识通常与肉身生命取向的认同感消失了。（停顿。）你所认为的影像，是基于你自身的神经结构以及你对它们的诠释。例如，当你考虑死后存续时，你会想象所有感官都在充分运作，尽管或许是在一个非物质的躯体中。在那样的脉络下，没有影像的感知似乎是不可能的。然而，在某些梦境情境中，你进入一种与那种感官数据完全分离的觉知状态。影像本身并未涉入，尽管之后它们可能为了翻译之故而于无意识中被制造出来。在这些状态中，你接近于理解你的意识在完全不具肉身取向时是什么样子。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Those who imagine they look upon nature with the most objective of eyes are those whose subjective beliefs blind them most of all, for they cannot see through their own misinterpretations. It has been said that statistics can be made to say two things at once, both contradictory; so the facts of nature can be read in completely different fashions as they are put together with the organizational abilities of the mind operating through the brain's beliefs. The exterior core of dreams is also blemished to that degree, but the inner core of dreams provides a constant new influx of material, feedback, and insight from the psyche, so that the personality is not at the mercy of its exterior experience only -- not confined to environmental feedback only, but ever provided with fresh intuitive data and direction.
quotes.translation: 那些想象自己以最客观的眼光看待自然的人，正是那些主观信念最令他们盲目的人，因为他们无法看穿自己的误解。常言道，统计数据可以同时说明两件相互矛盾的事；同样，自然的事实也可以以完全不同的方式被解读，当它们经由透过大脑信念运作的心灵组织能力被组合起来时。梦境的外在核心也受到同等程度的玷污，但梦境的内在核心则不断提供来自心灵的新材料、反馈与洞见，使得人格不为其外在经验所摆布——不局限于环境反馈，而是始终获得新鲜的直觉数据与指引。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Children experiment with the creation of joyful and frightening events, trying to ascertain for themselves the nature of their control over their own experience. They imagine joyful and terrifying experiences. They are in fact fascinated by the effects that their thoughts, feelings, and purposes have upon daily events. This is a natural learning process. If they create "bogeymen," then they can cause them to disappear also. If their thoughts can cause them to become ill, then there is no real reason for them to fear illness, for it is their own creation. This learning process is nipped in the bud, however. By the time you are adults, it certainly seems that you are a subjective being in an objective universe, at the mercy of others, and with only the most superficial control over the events of your lives.3
quotes.translation: 儿童实验着创造快乐与可怕的事件，试图为自己确认他们对自身经验掌控的本质。他们想象快乐与恐怖的经历。事实上，他们着迷于自己的思想、感受与目的对日常事件所产生的影响。这是一个自然的学习过程。如果他们创造了“妖怪”，那么他们也能让它们消失。如果他们的思想能让他们生病，那么他们就没有真正的理由害怕疾病，因为那是他们自己的创造。然而，这个学习过程在萌芽阶段就被扼杀了。等到你成年时，你当然会觉得自己是一个客观宇宙中的主观存在，受他人摆布，对自己生命中的事件只有最表面的掌控。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It is not enough to meditate, or to imagine in your mind some desired goal being accomplished, if you are afraid to act upon the very impulses to which your meditations and imaginings give rise. When you do not take any steps toward an ideal position, then your life does lack excitement. You become depressed. You might become an idealist in reverse, so that you find a certain excitement in contemplating the occurrence of natural disasters, such as earthquakes. (Pause.) You may begin to concentrate your attention on such activities. You may contemplate the end of the world instead, but in either case you are propelled by a sense of personal frustration, and perhaps by some degree of vengeance, seeing in your mind the destruction of a world that fell so far beneath your idealized expectations.
quotes.translation: 仅仅冥想或在心中想象某个渴望的目标得以实现是不够的，如果你害怕依照你的冥想和想象所激发的冲动去行动的话。当你没有朝着理想状态迈出任何步伐时，你的生活确实缺乏刺激。你会变得沮丧。你可能会成为一个反向的理想主义者，以至于在思考自然灾害（如地震）的发生时找到某种刺激。（停顿）你可能会开始将注意力集中在这样的活动上。你可能会转而思考世界末日，但无论哪种情况，你都是被个人挫败感所驱使，或许还有某种程度的报复心理，在脑海中看到一个远低于你理想化期望的世界的毁灭。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Over two years ago, in Note 2 for Session 801, and in the opening notes for Session 805, I described our decision to add the writing room to the house. All four of us involved in the moving were quite amused, then -- for the friends who helped Jane and me carry the furniture yesterday are the father-and-son contracting team that built the room in the first place.
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: There is great traffic flow in a city: A body knows how to leap out of the way in a moment's time from an approaching car. In the interior physical environment there is far greater traffic flow. There are decisions made in periods of time so brief you cannot imagine them--reactions that are almost over before they begin, reactions so fast you cannot perceive them as the body responds to its inner reality, and to all the stimuli from the exterior environment. The body is an open system. As solid as it seems to you, there are constant chemical reactions between it and the world, electromagnetic adjustments, alterations of balance, changes of relationships--alterations that occur between the body and its relationship with every other physical event, from the position of the planets and moon and the sun, to the position of the smallest grain of sand, to the tiniest microbe in anyone's intestine (intently).
quotes.translation: 城市中有巨大的交通流量：身体知道如何在瞬间从一辆驶近的汽车前跳开。在内在的物理环境中，有远为更大的交通流量。有在如此短暂的时间段内做出的决定，你无法想象它们——那些几乎在开始前就结束的反应，那些快到你无法察觉的反应，因为身体回应着它的内在实相，以及来自外在环境的所有刺激。身体是一个开放的系统。尽管它在你看来如此坚实，但它与世界之间存在着持续的化学反应、电磁调整、平衡的改变、关系的改变——这些改变发生在身体与每一个其他物理事件的关系中，从行星、月亮和太阳的位置，到最小沙粒的位置，再到任何人肠道中最微小的微生物（专注地）。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Those "properties" are the faculties of the imagination, creativity, telepathy, clairvoyance, and dreaming, as well as the functions of logic and reason. You know that you dream. You know that you think. Those are direct experiences. (Pause.) Anytime you use instruments to probe into the nature of reality, you are looking at a kind of secondary evidence, no matter how excellent the instruments may be. The subjective evidence of dreaming, for example, is far more "convincing" and irrefutable than is the evidence for an expanding universe, black holes, or even atoms and molecules themselves. Although instruments can indeed be most advantageous in many ways, they still present you with secondary rather than primary tools of investigation--and they distort the nature of reality far more than the subjective attributes of thoughts, feelings, and intuitions do.
quotes.translation: 那些"属性"是想象力、创造力、心灵感应、千里眼和做梦的官能，以及逻辑和理性的功能。你知道你在做梦。你知道你在思考。这些都是直接体验。（停顿）任何时候你使用仪器来探究实相的本质，你都是在观察一种间接证据，无论这些仪器多么出色。例如，做梦的主观证据远比膨胀宇宙、黑洞甚至原子和分子本身的证据更"令人信服"和无可辩驳。尽管仪器在许多方面确实可以非常有利，但它们仍然呈现给你的是间接而非直接的探究工具——而且它们对实相本质的扭曲远大于思想、感受和直觉的主观属性。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I do not believe that Dunne understood this. There is no serialization as he imagined, after a certain point, simply because this progression of selves through various times in a serial fashion is no longer necessary. The selves reach a point which is not a theoretical point, but a particular mathematically existent point, whereby these times and selves simply become one, or in our terms, an entity. I must stress that individuality is never lost. But this is too complicated a subject to cover this evening. We have explained it rather adequately in terms of action however, and gestalts of selves do not imply a giving-up of individuality at all. It should be remembered here that reincarnation is simply a fact, and one which is not accepted by Priestley or Dunne.
quotes.translation: 我不认为邓恩理解了这一点。不存在他所想象的那种序列化，在某个点之后，仅仅是因为这种自我以序列方式穿越各种时间的进程不再必要。自我达到一个点，这不是一个理论上的点，而是一个特定的数学上存在的点，在这些点上，这些时间和自我简单地合而为一，或者用我们的术语来说，成为一个存有。我必须强调，个体性从未丧失。但这是一个太复杂的话题，今晚无法涵盖。然而，我们已经用行动的概念相当充分地解释了它，而自我的完形绝不意味着放弃个体性。这里应该记住，转世只是一个事实，一个普里斯特利和邓恩都不接受的事实。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Now I have told you that you may legitimately visit not only the past, present and future as it existed, or will exist in physical terms; but you may also visit realities which never existed in physical terms. In our earliest sessions I emphasized that the intensity regulated the duration of an experience. Now, many events that were only imagined and never took place, physically speaking, many such events still exist. They are simply not a part of your definition of reality. You may therefore visit a museum which was planned in the 16th century, but never built. Such a museum has a reality as real, you see, as the house in which you live. Ordinarily you only perceive physical reality. In projections you may visit other realities such as these also--which you may be tempted to call imaginary, but they are not.
quotes.translation: 现在我已经告诉过你，你不仅可以合法地访问过去、现在和未来，如它们在物理层面上存在或将要存在的那样；而且你还可以访问那些从未在物理层面上存在过的实相。在我们最早的课程中，我强调强度调节了体验的持续时间。现在，许多事件只是被想象过而从未在物理上发生过，许多这样的事件仍然存在。它们只是不属于你对实相的定义的一部分。因此，你可以访问一个在16世纪被规划但从未建造的博物馆。这样的博物馆具有与你居住的房子一样真实的实相，你看。通常你只感知物理实相。在投射中，你也可以访问其他这样的实相——你可能会倾向于称之为想象的，但它们并非如此。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: He tries at times to use a schedule, a disciplined schedule as a fence against the subconscious, and becomes panic-stricken imagining himself without it. This is not to say that a schedule in itself is not good, it is to say that he can and sometimes does misuse one. In these instances the time is actually used to clamp down on the inner self, rather than to release it. These outings will be of benefit here, not only because they allow for spontaneous interaction, but because they serve to break the schedules in ways that do not disturb working hours. They provide a beginning for a habit of release. Here the ego is helping us now, for it is sticking to these plans, and the plans are those the ego itself enjoys, and can accept. He has been afraid to simply sit and think, for fear of letting the spontaneous self show without the symbolized writing mechanism.
quotes.translation: 他有时试图使用一个时间表，一个严格的时间表作为抵御潜意识的栅栏，并想象自己没有了它而惊慌失措。这并不是说时间表本身不好，而是说他可以并且有时确实会误用它。在这些情况下，时间实际上被用来压制内在自我，而不是释放它。这些外出活动将在此有益，不仅因为它们允许自发的互动，而且因为它们有助于以不干扰工作时间的方式打破时间表。它们为释放的习惯提供了一个开端。在这里，自我现在正在帮助我们，因为它坚持这些计划，而这些计划是自我本身享受并能接受的。他一直害怕只是坐下来思考，因为害怕让自发的自我在没有象征性写作机制的情况下显现出来。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Now. As I have told you, there is duration of a kind. What would correspond, in any case, to your idea of duration within the spacious present ; it is a matter of intensities however. Now the entity is in itself composed of such intensities. Simply for the sake of analogy, imagine the image, a humanoid one, of an entity giant-sized, spread out anywhere in your physical universe. And if the image were projected against a midnight sky, within its apparent boundaries then you would see a multitude of planets and stars. Let these represent moment points. On one hand they are a part of the entity, as your cells are a portion of your body. On the other hand the entity's consciousness can travel through these. They are doorways within his own psychological makeup, into experiences.
quotes.translation: 现在。正如我曾告诉你们的，存在一种持续性。无论如何，这对应于你们在广阔当下中对持续性的概念；然而这关乎强度。现在，存有本身由这些强度构成。仅为了类比，想象一个巨人尺寸的类人形象，散布在你们物质宇宙的任何地方。如果这个形象投射在午夜的天空中，在其明显的边界内，你们会看到众多行星和恒星。让这些代表时刻点。一方面，它们是存有的一部分，就像你们的细胞是你们身体的一部分。另一方面，存有的意识可以穿越这些时刻点。它们是他自身心理结构中的门道，通往各种体验。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: I do not mean to mislead you. Basic reality is not by nature terrifying. It is however vibrant and direct to the extreme, and you must be prepared before you experience such an ultrasonic action. A direct experience of reality involves complete use of all the inner senses to an integrated cognizance field. A very weak analogy can be found if you imagine at one time hearing the most exciting and moving music imaginable, while simultaneously smelling the strongest but not necessarily unpleasant odor, viewing the most emotionally-charged scene while feeling intense and vivid bodily sensations. In normal life you even turn down outer-sense stimuli for the sake of simplicity, and to enable you to focus upon those stimuli close at hand.
quotes.translation: 我并非有意误导你们。基本实相本质上并不可怕。然而它充满活力且极度直接，你们必须做好准备才能体验这种超音速行动。对实相的直接体验涉及所有内在感官的完整运用，形成一个整合的认知场。一个非常微弱的类比是：想象你同时听到最激动人心、最动人的音乐，闻到最强烈但不一定难闻的气味，观看最情感充溢的场景，同时感受强烈而鲜明的身体感觉。在正常生活中，你们甚至为了简化而降低外在感官的刺激，以便专注于手边的刺激。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Here I would like to give a very simple example, evocative of what I mean. The other day Ruburt received a telephone call from a woman in California who was in difficulty. Ruburt promised to send [healing] energy. Hanging up, he closed his eyes and imagined energy being sent out from a universal source through his own body, and directed toward the person in need. When he did so, Ruburt mentally saw a long "heavy" beam extending straight to the west from a point between his eyes. It reached without impediment. He felt that this extension was composed of energy, and it seemed so strong that a person could walk upon it without difficulty. Subjectively he felt that this beam of energy reached its destination. And so it did.6
quotes.translation: 这里我想给出一个非常简单的例子，以唤起我的意思。前几天，鲁柏接到一位加州女士的电话，她正陷入困境。鲁柏答应发送[疗愈]能量。挂断电话后，他闭上眼睛，想象能量从一个宇宙源头通过他的身体发送出去，并导向那位需要帮助的人。当他这样做时，鲁柏在脑海中看到一束长长的"沉重"光束从他两眼之间的一点直直向西延伸。它毫无阻碍地到达。他感觉这束延伸物由能量构成，看起来如此强大，以至于一个人可以毫不费力地走在上面。主观上，他觉得这束能量到达了目的地。事实也确实如此。

quotes.text: Many have seen that inner world as the source for the physical one, but imagined that man's purpose was merely to construct physically these perfect images to the best of his abilities. (Very forcefully:) In that picture man himself did not help create that inner world, or have any hand in its beauty. He could at best try to duplicate it physically -- never able, however, to match its perfection in those terms. In such a version of inner-outer reality the back-and-forth mobility, the give-and-take between inner and outer, is ignored. Man, being a part of that inner world by reason of the nature of his own psyche, automatically has a hand in the creation of those blueprints which at another level he uses as guides.
quotes.translation: 许多人将内在世界视为物质世界的源头，但想象人类的目的是尽最大努力在物质上构建这些完美的形象。（非常有力地：）在那幅图景中，人类自己并未帮助创造那个内在世界，也未参与其美丽。他最多只能尝试在物质上复制它——但永远无法在这些方面达到其完美。在这种内外实相的版本中，内外之间的来回流动性、相互给予被忽视了。人类，由于其自身心灵的本质，是那个内在世界的一部分，自动参与了那些蓝图的创造，而这些蓝图在另一个层面被用作指南。

quotes.text: Putting this as simply as possible, your actual experience is far too vast for you to physically follow. Your particular kind of consciousness is the result of specialized focus within a particular area. You imagine it to be "absolute," in that it seems to involve an all-exclusive state that includes your identity as you think of it -- only you give it boundaries like a kingdom. It is, instead, a certain kind of organization that is indeed inviolate even while it is itself a portion of other kinds of consciousnesses, with their own points of focus. Your body itself is composed of self-aware organizations of consciousness that escape your notice and deal with perceptual material utterly alien to your own ways.
quotes.translation: 尽可能简单地说，你们的实际体验过于广阔，以至于你们无法在物质上追随。你们特定的意识类型是在特定区域内专门聚焦的结果。你们想象它是"绝对的"，因为它似乎涉及一个包罗万象的状态，包含你们所认为的身份——只是你们给它划定了像王国一样的边界。相反，它是一种特定的组织，即使它本身是其他类型意识的一部分，拥有自己的焦点，它仍然是不可侵犯的。你们的身体本身由自我意识的意识组织构成，这些组织逃过你们的注意，处理与你们方式完全不同的感知材料。

quotes.text: The imagination can vaguely perceive, of course, some probabilities, but the physical organism can directly experience but one of these within physical time, and in terms of continuity. The probable events however are precisely as real as that one event which is chosen from them to be a physical experience. And these events therefore become "real", in quotes, within other dimensions. As a sideline here, there are some interesting episodes, not at all understood, when a severe psychological shock, or even a deep sense of unendurable futility, will cause a short circuit, so to speak, so that one portion of the self becomes aware, and begins to experience reality as it exists for another portion of the self.
quotes.translation: 当然，想象力可以模糊地感知某些可能性，但物质有机体只能在物质时间内，以连续性的方式直接体验其中之一。然而，可能的事件与从中被选为物质体验的那个事件一样真实。因此，这些事件在其他维度中成为"真实的"，带引号。作为附带说明，有一些有趣的插曲，完全不被理解，当严重的心理冲击，甚至深度的无法忍受的徒劳感，会导致所谓的短路，使得自我的一部分开始意识到，并开始体验实相，如同它为自我的另一部分存在那样。

quotes.text: Your main sense of identity is involved with your physical body, so that it is, for example, extremely difficult for you to imagine yourself without it, or outside it, or in any way disconnected from it. Form is the result of concentrated energy, the pattern for it caused by vividly directed emotional or psychic idea images. The intensity is all important. If you have, for example, a highly vivid desire to be somewhere else, then without realizing it consciously a pseudophysical form, identical with your own, may appear in that very spot. The desire will carry the imprint of your personality and image, even though you remain unaware of the image or its appearance in the other location.
quotes.translation: 你主要的身份认同与你的肉体紧密相连，以至于你极难想象自己没有肉体、或处于肉体之外、或以任何方式与肉体分离。形态是能量集中的结果，其模式由强烈导向的情感或心灵意念影像所引发。强度至关重要。举例来说，若你极度渴望身处别处，那么在你未察觉的情况下，一个与你自身完全相同的拟似物理形态可能会出现在那个地点。这份渴望会携带你人格与形象的印记，即便你始终未意识到该影像或其出现在另一位置的事实。

quotes.text: Good evening to our friend. Now, to our fine young lady: you must watch the pictures that you paint with your imagination, for you allow your imagination too full a reign. If you read our early material, you will see that your environment and the conditions of your life at any given time are the direct result of your own expectations. You form physical materiaizationls of these realities within your own mind.
quotes.translation: 晚上好，我们的朋友。现在，对我们这位优秀的年轻女士说：你必须留意你用想象力描绘的画面，因为你让想象力过于自由驰骋。若你阅读我们早期的资料，便会发现你在任何特定时刻的环境与生活状况，皆是你自身期望的直接结果。你在自己的心智中形成了这些实相的物质显化。

quotes.text: The emotions and the imagination, however, give you your closest contact with other portions of your own reality. They also liberate your intellect so that its powers are not limited by concepts it has been taught are true. Instead, such concepts are relatively true -- operationally true. For example, the physical laws that you are familiar with operate where you are. They are true, relatively speaking. In those terms you are one person physically objectified, staring upward in the scene just mentioned at an objectified sky. You weigh so many pounds, tilt your head at such-and-such an angle to peer upward at the skyscape, and physically speaking, you can be categorized.
quotes.translation: 然而，情感与想象力让你得以最紧密地接触你自身实相的其他部分。它们也解放了你的智力，使其力量不再受限于那些被教导为真理的概念。相反，这些概念只是相对真理——操作上的真理。例如，你所熟悉的物理法则在你所在之处运作。相对而言，它们是真实的。就这些条件而言，你是物质化的个体，在上述场景中仰望物质化的天空。你体重若干磅，以特定角度仰头凝视天景，从物理层面来说，你可以被归类。

quotes.text: Now on the question of "mental disorders," it is highly important that individual integrity be stressed, rather than the blanket definitions that are usually accorded to any group of symptoms. In many such circumstances, however, such individuals are combining the imagination and the reasoning abilities in ways that are not in keeping with their historic periods. (With some irony:) It would not be entirely out of keeping, though somewhat exaggerated a statement, to claim that men who stockpile nuclear weapons in order to preserve peace are insane. In your society, such activities are, in a way that completely escapes me, somehow under the label of humanitarianism!
quotes.translation: 现在关于"精神障碍"的问题，强调个体的完整性极为重要，而非通常赋予任何一组症状的笼统定义。然而在许多此类情况下，这些个体正以不符合其历史时期的方式结合想象力与推理能力。（略带讽刺地说：）声称那些为维护和平而囤积核武器的人精神失常，虽有些夸张，却也并非完全不合情理。在你的社会中，这类活动竟以某种我完全无法理解的方式，被冠以人道主义的标签！

quotes.text: You cannot react physically in the same way to projected or imagined dangers. There seems to be no possible reaction. You are frustrated. You are meant to deal with your immediate, primary experience, and in so doing you take care of your responsibility. You are able to take action in your own experience, and therefore affect others. You do not have to be ignorant of wars in other corners of the world, or close your eyes. But if you allow those experiences to overcloud your present, valid intersection with reality, then you speak and act from a position not your own, and deny the world whatever benefits your own present version of reality might allow you to give.
quotes.translation: 你无法以同样的身体方式对投射或想象的危险做出反应。似乎没有可能的反应方式。你感到挫败。你本应处理你直接、首要的经验，如此你便尽到了责任。你能够在自己的经验中采取行动，从而影响他人。你不必对世界其他角落的战争视而不见，也不必闭上眼睛。但若你让那些经验遮蔽你当下与实相的有效交集，那么你便从并非自身的位置说话与行动，并否定了世界可能从你当下实相版本中获益的一切。

quotes.text: As you read this book, now and then look about you at the room in which you sit. Chairs and tables, the ceilings and the floors, may seem very real and solid -- quite permanent -- while you by contrast may feel yourself to be highly vulnerable, caught in a moment between birth and extinction. You may even feel jealous when you think of it, imagining that the physical universe will continue to exist long after you are gone. By the end of our book, however, I hope you will realize the eternal validity of your own consciousness, and the impermanence of those physical aspects of your environment, and of your universe, that now seem so secure. Do you have that?
quotes.translation: 当你阅读本书时，请不时环顾你所在的房间。桌椅、天花板与地板，可能显得非常真实而坚固——相当持久——而相比之下，你或许觉得自己极为脆弱，被困在出生与消亡之间的瞬间。你甚至可能对此感到嫉妒，想象在你离世后，物质宇宙仍将长久存在。然而，到本书结束时，我希望你将意识到你自身意识的永恒有效性，以及你环境与宇宙中那些看似稳固的物质面向的无常性。你明白了吗？

quotes.text: Many individuals imagine the soul to be an immortalized ego, forgetting that the ego as you know it is only a small portion of the self; so this section of the personality is simply projected onward, ad infinitum, so to speak. Because the dimensions of your reality are so little understood, your concepts are bound to be limited. In considering "immortality," mankind seems to hope for further egotistical development, and yet he objects to the idea that such development might involve change. He says through his religions that he has a soul indeed, without even asking what a soul is, and often he seems to regard it, again, as an object in his possession.
quotes.translation: 许多人将灵魂想象为不朽的自我，却忘了你们所知的自我只是本体的一小部分；因此人格的这一部分只是被无限地投射向前，可以这么说。由于你们对实相的维度知之甚少，你们的概念必然受限。在思考"不朽"时，人类似乎希望获得进一步的自我发展，却又反对这种发展可能涉及改变的想法。他通过宗教宣称自己确实拥有灵魂，却连灵魂是什么都未曾追问，而且常常再次将其视为自己拥有的一个物件。

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... Historically, of course, you follow a one-line pattern of thought, so you see a picture in which fish left the oceans and became reptiles; from these mammals eventually appeared, and apes and men. That is, I admit, a simple statement, but it is the way most people think evolution occurred. The terms of "progression" are tricky. You never imagine the situation being reversed, for example. Few of you ever imagine a conscious reptilian man. It seems to you that the direction you took is the only direction that could have been taken.
quotes.translation: 请给我们一点时间……从历史上看，你们当然遵循一条直线的思维模式，所以你们看到一幅画面：鱼类离开海洋变成爬行动物；从这些爬行动物中最终出现了哺乳动物、猿类和人类。我承认，这是一个简单的陈述，但这是大多数人认为进化发生的方式。"进步"这个术语很棘手。你们从未想象过情况会反过来，例如。你们中很少有人想象过一个有意识的爬行动物人。在你们看来，你们所走的方向是唯一可能的方向。

quotes.text: The origin is not in others. For fifteen minutes a day agree to suspend critical judgment, and following the Maltz method imagine yourself vividly in the position you would like to be. The rest of the time consciously make an effort to control your attitude when you find yourself thinking in terms of want or poverty. Then switch your thoughts to ideas of plenty. This need not involve hypocrisy, indeed must not. You must feel that this is legitimate and practical, because the thoughts of plenty will automatically begin to attract plenty. This is sufficient. There should not be an overemphasis (underlined) on material possessions or security however.
quotes.translation: 起源不在他人身上。每天花十五分钟暂停批判性判断，按照马尔茨的方法生动地想象自己处于理想的位置。其余时间，当你发现自己以匮乏或贫穷的念头思考时，有意识地努力控制自己的态度。然后将思绪转向丰盛的想法。这不必涉及虚伪，事实上绝不能。你必须感到这是合理且实际的，因为丰盛的想法会自动开始吸引丰盛。这就足够了。不过，不应过度强调物质财富或安全感。

quotes.text: Even if a direct solution does not appear, rejuvenation will of itself begin to point you in the proper direction. If you are a woman in an unhappy marriage, for example, you may begin by imagining yourself with a fine suitor. Now: No Sir Galahad may appear, but if the exercise is pursued properly you will automatically begin to feel loved, and therefore worthy of love, and lovable, where before you felt rejected, unworthy and inferior. This feeling of being loved will alter your reality, drawing love to you. You will act loved. Your spouse may then find you exhibiting characteristics of a most pleasant nature, and he himself may change.
quotes.translation: 即使直接解决方案没有出现，更新本身也会开始将你引向正确的方向。例如，如果你是一个身处不幸婚姻中的女人，你可以从想象自己有一位优秀的追求者开始。现在：可能不会有加拉哈德爵士出现，但如果练习得当，你会自动开始感到被爱，因此值得被爱且可爱，而之前你感到被拒绝、不值得和自卑。这种被爱的感觉会改变你的实相，将爱吸引到你身边。你会表现得像个被爱的人。你的配偶可能会发现你展现出最令人愉快的特质，他自己也可能改变。

quotes.text: On the other hand your intuitions follow a different kind of organization, as does your imagination -- one involved with associations, an organization that unifies diverse elements and brings even known events together in a kind of unity that is often innocent of the limitations dictated by cause and effect. In those terms, then, Framework 2 deals with associations, so that within it the recognizable events of the physical world can be put together in an infinite number of ways, after which they appear in your private experience according to directions you have given them through those associations that you form mentally.
quotes.translation: 另一方面，你的直觉遵循一种不同的组织方式，你的想象力也是如此——一种涉及联想、统一不同元素并将已知事件以某种统一性结合起来的组织方式，这种统一性往往不受因果关系的限制。因此，用那些术语来说，框架二处理联想，因此在其中，物质世界的可识别事件可以以无限多种方式组合，之后它们根据你通过心智形成的那些联想所给予的方向，出现在你的个人经验中。

They were indeed as agile as animals -- nor were they unconscious.10 They simply dealt with a different kind of awareness.
quotes.translation: 它像动物一样，被装备得能在其环境中完美运作。你会称它为无心智的，因为它似乎不会推理。仅就本次讨论而言，想象一个拥有完全运作的身体意识的身体，没有任何疾病或因出生缺陷，但没有你们所拥有的那种主导的自我导向意识。曾有具有这种性质的物种。用你们的术语来说，它们看起来像梦游者，然而它们的身体能力超越了你们。它们确实像动物一样敏捷——也并非无意识。它们只是处理一种不同的觉知。

quotes.translation: 建议你入睡时并非陷入沉睡，而是进入另一种清醒状态。试着想象自己在睡眠中保持清醒。另一些时候，当你上床躺下安顿好自己，在入睡时想象自己正在次日清晨醒来。我不会告诉你该寻找什么。这些练习的本身才是重要的——而非通常意义上的结果。

quotes.text: When you consider ideas as mental and apart from nature, then you feel separated from nature itself. When you imagine a life after death as unnatural or supernatural then you feel divorced, cut off and bewildered. You must try to understand that there are different kinds of nature within Nature -- and a capital for the last one. Your physical life -- your human nature -- is, in your terms, dependent upon a time when you were not. You must realize that not being in that connotation is quite as natural as physical being. Your existence before and after death is as much a normal phenomenon as your present life.
quotes.translation: 当你将思想视为纯粹心智的、与自然分离的事物时，你便感到与自然本身割裂。当你将死后生命想象为不自然或超自然时，你便感到疏离、隔绝与困惑。你必须明白，在自然之内存在着不同种类的自然——后者需用大写字母表示。就你们而言，你们的物质生命——你们的人性——依赖于一个你们尚未存在的时期。你必须认识到，在那个语境中不存在与物质存在一样自然。你们生前与死后的存在，与你们当前的生命一样是正常现象。

quotes.text: The "you" who is capable of such expansion must be a far more creative and multidimensional personality than you earlier imagined. Many of the suggested small exercises given earlier in the book will also help you become acquainted with your own reality, will give you direct experience with the nature of your own soul or entity, and will put you in contact with those portions of your being from which your own vitality springs. You may or may not have your own encounters with past reincarnational selves or probable selves. You may or may not catch yourselves in the act of changing levels of consciousness.

quotes.text: The subconscious however is not so limited. If you consider the ego at the apex of the moment point, and imprisoned therefore within the realm of its own before-and-after, cause-and-effect experience, then you can imagine the subconscious reaching further outward and seizing upon many other moment points. It should be easy to see then why the focus of the ego is so sharp and brilliant. Within its limited scope there is intensity of stimuli and response. Indeed, the ego is that portion of the personality which is plunged into a specific and intense preoccupation with a given field of action or dimension.
quotes.translation: 然而潜意识并非如此受限。如果你将自我视为处于当下时刻点的顶点，因而被囚禁在它自身的前后因果经验领域内，那么你可以想象潜意识向外延伸，抓住许多其他时刻点。这样便容易理解为何自我的焦点如此锐利明亮。在其有限范围内，刺激与反应具有强烈强度。事实上，自我正是人格中投入特定且强烈专注于某一行动或维度领域的那部分。

quotes.text: The first "object" was an almost unendurable mass, though it had no weight, and it exploded, instantaneously beginning processes that formed the universe--but no time was involved. The process that you might imagine took up eons occurred in the twinkling of an eye, and the initial objective materialization of the massive thought of All That Is burst into reality. In your terms this was a physical explosion--but in the terms of the consciousnesses involved in that breakthrough, this was experienced as a triumphant "first" inspirational frenzy, a breakthrough into another kind of being (most intently).
quotes.translation: 第一个"物体"几乎是难以承受的团块，尽管它没有重量，它爆炸了，瞬间启动了形成宇宙的过程——但其中没有时间参与。你可能想象耗费了亿万年的过程，实则发生在眨眼之间，而"一切万有"那庞大思想的初始客观物质化，爆发进入实相。以你们的说法，这是一次物理爆炸——但从参与那次突破的意识角度而言，这被体验为一次胜利的"首次"灵感狂潮，一次突破进入另一种存在的形态（极为专注地）。

quotes.text: They certainly have a reality in energy, and they aid in the conversion of energy into physical terms. They are active rather than passive, then. You see about you physical forces and think nothing of them. For example: You feel the wind and its effects, but you cannot see it. The wind itself is invisible. So these other forces are also invisible. In basic terms they are no more good or evil than the wind is. I say this because you usually imagine that if something is good, there must be a countering force that is evil. Such is not the case. In greater terms these forces are good. They are protective. They nourish every living thing. They have been the impetus for what you think of as evolution. They are biological in that they are to some extent composed of mass cellular knowledge -- basically free of time, but directing physical activity in time, and thereby maintaining physical equilibrium.
quotes.translation: 它们在能量中确实具有真实性，并协助将能量转化为物质形式。因此它们是主动而非被动的。你看到周围的物质力量却不以为意。例如：你能感受到风及其影响，却看不见它。风本身是不可见的。这些其他力量同样不可见。从根本上说，它们与风一样无所谓善恶。我这样说是因为你们通常认为，如果某事物是善的，就必然存在与之对抗的恶势力。事实并非如此。从更高层面看，这些力量是善的。它们具有保护性。它们滋养着一切生灵。它们一直是你们所谓进化的原动力。它们具有生物性，因为在一定程度上由大量细胞知识构成——基本上不受时间束缚，却在时间中指导物质活动，从而维持物质平衡。

quotes.text: Using your conscious mind as a threshold, however, you can discover still more. Figuratively speaking, stand where you are. Think of that moment of conscious awareness as a path. Imagine many other such paths, all converging; again, imaginatively take one of them in your mind and follow it. Accept what you experience uncritically. To some small extent you are "altering" your consciousness. (Half humorously:) Of course, you are not "altering" it at all. You are simply using it in a different fashion, and focusing it -- however briefly -- in another direction. This is the simplest of exercises.
quotes.translation: 然而，以你的意识心为门槛，你可以发现更多。打个比方，站在原地。将那个意识觉知的时刻视为一条路径。想象许多其他这样的路径，全部汇聚在一起；再次，在想象中选取其中一条，跟随它。不加评判地接受你所体验的一切。在某种程度上，你正在"改变"你的意识。（半开玩笑地：）当然，你根本没有"改变"它。你只是以不同的方式使用它，并将它聚焦——无论多么短暂——于另一个方向。这是最简单的练习。

quotes.text: In a fashion, the intellect goes hand-in-hand with the imagination under such conditions. It is not that man stressed physical data less, but that he put it together differently -- that in the darkness he relied upon his inner and outer senses in a more unified fashion. The nightly portions of your personalities have become strangers to you -- for as you identify with what you think of as your rational intellect, then you identify it further with the daytime hours, with the objective world that becomes visible in the morning, with the clearcut physical objects that are then before your view.

quotes.text: I make these analogies because they are pertinent, yet I am aware that they can make you feel small or fear for your identity. In a book we must use words, but such analogies can, if you let them, conjure up within your imagination some feeling of your intimate relationship with all other reality. To some extent, the feeling of grace is your emotional recognition of the necessity, purpose and freedom, the innate appreciation, of your rightness and your place in existence.
quotes.translation: 我作这些类比是因为它们切题，然而我意识到它们可能让你感到渺小或为你的身份认同而担忧。你不仅仅是一则信息，比如，穿过超我广阔领域的信息。你并未迷失在宇宙中。在一本书中我们必须使用文字，但如果你允许，这些类比可以在你想象中唤起你与所有其他实相亲密关系的感受。在某种程度上，恩宠感是你对必要性、目的与自由的情感认知，是对你存在之正当性与位置的内在欣赏。

quotes.text: The deeply and strongly dimensioned sphere I used as an analogy for an action, if you recall, for any portion of action; you can now indeed further imagine one entity being composed of such an action, with egos like many faces looking outward in all directions, and each perceiving vastly different fields of reality; looking inward and outward, backward and forward as it were, through and beyond. And yet each action, or entity, is a part of another, and is both within and without another. And none of it is meaningless, and yet in a basic manner all of it has the meaning that you give it.
quotes.translation: 我曾用那个具有深度与强维度的球体来类比一个行动，如果你记得，它适用于任何行动部分；你现在确实可以进一步想象一个存有由这样的行动构成，具有许多如面孔般的自我向各个方向外观，每个都感知到截然不同的实相领域；向内与向外、向后与向前观看，仿佛穿透并超越一切。然而每个行动或存有，都是另一个的一部分，既在另一个之内，又在另一个之外。这一切都非无意义，然而从根本上说，它的一切意义都由你赋予。

quotes.text: Instead, you have put love into very definite categories, so that its existence is right only under the most limited conditions. Love goes underground, but springs up in distorted forms and exaggerated tendencies. You have followed this course for different reasons at different times. Neither sex is to blame. Instead your sexual situation is simply another reflection of the state of your consciousness. As a species, presently at least in the Western world, you equate sex and love. You imagine that sexual expression is the only one natural to love. Love, in other words, must it seems express itself exclusively through the exploration (humorously and deeper), in one way or another, of the beloved's sexual portions.
quotes.translation: 相反，你们将爱划分成非常明确的类别，以至于它的存在只在最有限的条件下才是正当的。爱转入地下，却以扭曲的形式和夸大的倾向涌现。你们在不同时期出于不同原因遵循这条路线。两性都不应受指责。相反，你们的性状况只是你们意识状态的另一反映。作为一个物种，至少在西方世界，你们将性与爱等同。你们想象性表达是爱唯一自然的方式。换言之，爱似乎必须通过探索（幽默而深入地）所爱之人的性部位，以某种方式才能完全表达自己。

quotes.text: In daily practical experience, try to concentrate for a while upon seemingly subordinate abilities, ones that you think of as latent. quotes.translation: 在日常实际经验中，试着暂时专注于看似次要的能力，那些你认为处于潜伏状态的能力。如果你持续这样做，运用你的想象力和意志，那么这些能力将在你的当下变得突出。当前的信念会重新编程并改变过去的经验。这不仅仅是过去被遗忘、无意识感知的事件会以新方式组合并归入新类别，而是在那个（现在不可感知的）过去中，整个身体对看似过去事件的反应都会改变。

quotes.text: He heard tomorrow's weather report (yesterday), groaned, thought of a very uncomfortable 90-degree temperature tomorrow [and] imagined himself miserable with the heat. Indeed, he began to feel warmer. In a flash he remembered previous days of discomfort, and in the next moment he projected those into the weekend. He felt trapped. Midway through this process he tried to catch himself, but he believed that his body could not handle the heat -- and that belief outweighed his intent to change his thoughts, so they kept returning for perhaps ten minutes.
quotes.translation: 他（昨天）听到了明天的天气预报，呻吟着，想到明天令人不适的90华氏度高温，想象自己在炎热中痛苦不堪。确实，他开始感觉更热了。一瞬间他记起过去不适的日子，下一刻他将这些投射到周末。他感到被困住了。在这个过程中途他试图控制自己，但他相信自己的身体无法承受高温——这个信念压倒了他改变想法的意图，所以思绪持续回返了大约十分钟。

quotes.text: For now, consider a very simple act. You want to walk across the room and pick up a paper, for example. That purpose is simple and direct enough. It automatically propels your body in the proper fashions, even though you are not consciously aware of the inner mechanisms involved. You do not imagine the existence of blocks or impediments in your way, in the form of additional furniture placed in your path by accident, fate, or design. You make a simple straight path in the proper direction. The act has meaning because it is something you want to do.
quotes.translation: 现在，考虑一个非常简单的动作。例如，你想穿过房间捡起一张纸。这个目的足够简单直接。它自动以适当方式驱动你的身体，即使你没有有意识地意识到其中涉及的内在机制。你不会想象路径上存在障碍或阻碍——那些由偶然、命运或设计额外放置在家具形式的障碍。你朝着正确方向走出一条简单的直线路径。这个动作有意义，因为这是你想做的事。

quotes.translation: 你不能如此字面地对待思想和想象力，在很大层面上也不应试图"守护你的思想"，仿佛它们是你要保持纯种繁殖的牲畜群。你的思想确实形成你的现实。然而，如果你不恐惧它们，它们会创造自身的平衡。心灵栖居的现实与你通常认知的世界如此不同，以至于在那里，你所认为的善恶也被视为操作性或相对性的真实，如同感知者与被感知对象之间的差异一样。

quotes.text: If you will think （I hope） for simplicity's sake of the whole self as it exists on your plane with its physical body, conscious ego and inner self as one field unit, which is also part of the larger or more complete entity as one field unit within another, then perhaps it will not be too much for you to imagine the connection, or one of the connections, between the entity field and the whole-self field, which is on your plane as being the inner senses--that is, the inner senses are one of the connectives between these two fields.
quotes.translation: 如果你愿意（我希望）为了简单起见，将存在于你们层面上的整个自己——包括其物质身体、意识自我和内在自己——视为一个场域单位，它同时也是更大或更完整的存有的一部分，如同另一个场域内的一个场域单位，那么你或许就不难想象存有场域与整个自己场域之间的连接，或其中一个连接，在你们层面上就是内在感官——也就是说，内在感官是这两个场域之间的连接物之一。

quotes.text: With splendid innocence and exuberant pride, you imagine that the evolutionary system as you know it is the only one, that physically there can be no more. Now within the physical reality that you know, there are hints and clues as to the nature of other physical realities. There are, latent, within your own physical forms other senses, unused, that could have come to the front but in your probability did not. Now I have been speaking of earthly developments, realities therefore clustered about earthly aspects as you know them.
quotes.translation: 带着绝妙的天真和旺盛的骄傲，你想象你们所知的演化系统是唯一的，物质上不可能再有更多。然而，在你们所知的物质实相中，存在着关于其他物质实相本质的暗示和线索。在你们自己的物质形态中，潜伏着其他未被使用的感官，它们本可以显现，但在你们的概率中却没有。现在，我一直在谈论地球上的发展，因此这些实相都围绕着你们所知的地球面向聚集。

quotes.text: Imagine vividly what you will do tomorrow, and in detail plan a probable day that will rise naturally from your present experience, behavior, and purposes. Follow through as you did with the first part of the exercise. (Pause.) That day's reality is already anticipated by your cells. Your body has prepared for it, all of its functions precognitively projecting their own existences into it. Your "future" life exists in the same manner, and in your terms grows as much out of your present as tomorrow grows out of your today.
quotes.translation: 生动想象你明天将做什么，详细规划一个会从你当下经历、行为与目的中自然生发的可能的一天。像做练习第一部分那样贯彻到底。（停顿。）你细胞早已预期那天的实相。你的身体已为此准备，它所有功能都预知性地将自身存在投射其中。你的"未来"生命以同样方式存在，用你的话说，它从你的当下生长出来，正如明天从你的今天生长出来。

quotes.text: At the same time imagine that these creatures are really one creature, but with definite parts equipped to handle two entirely different worlds. At the same time, and this is the difficult part to explain, neither of the two faces would ever see the other world. They would not be aware of each other. And yet each would be fully self-conscious.
quotes.translation: 同时想象这些生物其实是同一个生物，但具有明确的部分装备来处理两个完全不同的世界。因此，在这个真正荒谬的类比中，潜意识会存在于两个大脑之间，使该生物能作为一个统一体运作。同时——这是最难解释的部分——两张脸永远不会看见对方的世界。它们不会察觉彼此。然而每张脸都完全具有自我意识。

But while disasters, imagined or encountered second-handedly, may in fact later occur, they are far different from physically encountered ones. You only add to their unfortunate nature by negatively brooding upon what might happen in the future, and you destroy your own stance. Your stance in time is highly important, for it is your practical base of operations.
quotes.translation: 恐怕我认为其中某些部分仍会令你们——指鲁柏、你及其他读者——感到费解。但灾难，无论是想象中或间接听闻的，即使后来可能确实发生，也与亲身经历的灾难截然不同。你只会因消极沉思未来可能发生之事而加剧其不幸本质，并摧毁自己的立足点。你在时间中的立足点至关重要，因为那是你实际运作的基础。

quotes.text: But what gives life to the egg or the seed now, keeps it going, provides that energy? Imagining some great big-bang theory (to explain the creation of the universe) gives you an immense explosion of energy, that somehow turns into life but must wear out somewhere along the line -- and if that were the case, life would be getting weaker all the time, but it is not. The child is as new and fresh today as a child was 5,000 years ago, and each spring is as new.
quotes.translation: 但究竟是什么赋予卵子或种子生命，使其持续运作，提供那股能量？想象某个伟大的大爆炸理论（用以解释宇宙的创生）会给你一场巨大的能量爆发，它不知何故转化为生命，却必然在某个过程中耗竭——倘若如此，生命应会不断衰弱，但事实并非如此。今日的孩童与五千年前的孩童一样崭新鲜活，每个春天都同样焕然一新。

quotes.text: You must therefore understand and examine your beliefs, realize that they form your experience, and consciously change those that do not give the effects you want. In such an examination you will be aware of many excellent beliefs that work for you. Trace these through. See how they were followed by your imagination and emotions. If possible, look in your own past for points where recognizable new ideas came to you and beneficially changed your experience.
quotes.translation: 因此你必须理解并检视你的信念，认识到它们塑造了你的经验，并有意识地改变那些无法带来你想要效果的信念。在此检视中，你会意识到许多为你效力的优秀信念。追溯它们的脉络，观察你的想象力与情感如何追随它们。若有可能，回顾自己的过去，找出那些可辨识的新观念降临并有益地改变你经验的时刻。

quotes.text: But though you met both the present Dick and Loren, neither of you knew what your relationship would be in this life. You intended a brother lifelong relationship with the man who is, instead, your father. Hence he passes you by, the bicycle being a symbol of youth. That is, because you imagined that he would be a contemporary in age, you saw him on a bicycle, a child's method of transportation, but because he was born earlier the vehicle carries him past.
quotes.translation: 但尽管你们同时遇见了现在的迪克和洛伦，你们俩都不知道这一世彼此的关系会如何。你们原本打算与那个实际上是你们父亲的男人建立终生的兄弟关系。因此他与你擦肩而过，自行车象征着青春。也就是说，因为你们想象他会在年龄上相仿，所以你们看见他骑着自行车——一种孩童的交通工具——但由于他出生得更早，这辆车载着他驶过了你们身边。

quotes.text: [Many of] the flying saucer appearances come from [such] a plane, [one] that is much more advanced in technological sciences than earth at this time. However, this is still not a mental-science plane. Therefore the camouflage paraphernalia appears, more or less visible, to your own astonishment. Now, so strong is this tendency for vitality to change from one apparent form to another, that what you have here in your flying object is something that is actually, as you view it, not of your plane or of [whatever] plane of its origin ... The atoms and molecules that structurally compose the UFO, and which are themselves formed by vitality, are more or less aligned according to the pattern of its own territory. Now as the craft enters your plane a distortion occurs. Its actual structure is caught in a dilemma of form ... between transforming itself completely into earth's particular camouflage pattern, and retaining its original pattern. The earthly viewer attempts to correlate what he sees with what he supposedly knows or imagines possible in the universe.

quotes.text: (11:41.) These strands are like double dreams that continue. They also serve as a framework to the recognized self. In periods of stress or challenge the recognized self may sense these other strains of consciousness, and realize that a fuller experience is possible, a greater psychological thickness. On some occasions in the dream state the recognized self may then enlarge its perception enough to take advantage of these other portions of its own identity. Double or triple dreams may represent such encounters at times. Consciousness always seeks the richest, most creative form, while ever maintaining its own integrity. The imagination, playing, the arts and dreaming, allow it to enrich its activities by providing feedback other than that received in the physical environment itself.
quotes.translation: （11:41。）这些线索就像持续的双重梦境。它们也为被认可的自己提供了框架。在压力或挑战时期，被认可的自己可能会感知到这些其他的意识流，并意识到更丰富的体验是可能的，一种更大的心理厚度。在某些情况下，在梦境状态中，被认可的自己可能会扩大其感知，足以利用其自身身份的这些其他部分。双重或三重梦境有时可能代表这样的相遇。意识总是在寻求最丰富、最具创造性的形式，同时始终维护自身的完整性。想象力、游戏、艺术和做梦，通过提供除了物理环境本身接收到的反馈之外的反馈，使其能够丰富其活动。

quotes.text: You dreamed you spoke languages before their physical invention, of course. It was the nature of your dreams, and your dreams' creativity, that made you what you are, for otherwise you would have developed a mechanical-like language -- had you developed one at all -- that named designations, locations, and dealt with the most simple, objective reality: "I walked there. He walks there. The sun is hot." You would not have had that kind of bare statement of physical fact. You would not have had (pause) any way (pause) of conceiving of objects that did not already exist. You would not have had any way of imagining yourselves in novel situations. You would not have had any overall picture of the seasons, for dreaming educated the memory and lengthened man's attention span. It reinforced the lessons of daily life, and was highly important in man's progress.
quotes.translation: 你梦见自己在语言被物理发明之前就说了那些语言，当然。正是你梦境的性质和梦境的创造力，造就了现在的你，否则你会发展出一种机械式的语言——如果你真的发展出语言的话——那种语言只命名指定、位置，并处理最简单、最客观的现实："我走到那里。他走到那里。太阳很热。" 你就不会有那种对物理事实的赤裸陈述。你就不会有（停顿）任何方式（停顿）去构想尚不存在的物体。你就不会有任何方式想象自己处于新奇的情境中。你就不会有季节的整体画面，因为梦境教育了记忆，延长了人类的注意力跨度。它强化了日常生活的教训，在人类的进步中极为重要。

quotes.text: But if the interactions between or among frameworks exist for everybody, in our terms, then as far as I'm concerned they exist for each thing as well--and I do mean the so-called "inanimate." (This isn't the place to go into it, but Seth maintains that for many reasons we arbitrarily decide what's living and nonliving.) Each reincarnational self, each counterpart self and probable self has its complement of frameworks. So does the most minute living or nonliving entity and the most gigantic. So, "probably," do most of the far-out probable realities one can imagine--for I won't go so far as to deny that some probable realities may exist without such framework structures. Strange one-dimensional "flatlands" indeed! But in each case where those framework interactions operate, they help each creation, each presence, each essence or vital principle fulfill "a larger picture that the conscious mind may not be able presently to perceive." In ways I can't even begin to describe here, all frameworks must ultimately be joined within the ineffable context of All That Is.
quotes.translation: 但如果框架之间的相互作用对每个人都存在，用我们的说法，那么在我看来，它们对每个事物也存在——我确实指的是所谓的"无生命"。（这里不是深入探讨的地方，但赛斯坚持认为，出于许多原因，我们武断地决定了什么是活的，什么是非活的。）每个转世自己、每个对应自己和每个可能的自己都有其框架的补充。最微小的有生命或无生命实体以及最庞大的实体也是如此。所以，"很可能"，大多数人们能想象到的遥远的可能实相也是如此——因为我不至于否认某些可能实相可能在没有这种框架结构的情况下存在。奇怪的一维"扁平世界"确实存在！但在每个框架相互作用运作的案例中，它们帮助每个创造、每个存在、每个本质或生命原则实现"一个意识心目前可能无法感知的更大画面"。以我在这里甚至无法开始描述的方式，所有框架最终都必须在一切万有的不可言喻的语境中结合在一起。

quotes.text: Give us a moment.... For what would seem to you to be eons, according to your time scale, men were in the dreaming state far more than they were in the waking one. They slept long hours, as did the animals--awakening, so to speak, to exercise their bodies, obtain sustenance, and, later, to mate. It was indeed a dreamlike world, but a highly charming and vital one, in which dreaming imaginations played rambunctiously with all the probabilities entailed in this new venture: imagining the various forms of language and communication possible, spinning great dream tales of future civilizations replete with their own built-in histories--building, because they were now allied with time, mental edifices that automatically created pasts as well as futures.
quotes.translation: 请给我们一点时间……按照你们的时间尺度，在看似漫长的岁月里，人类处于梦境状态的时间远多于清醒状态。他们像动物一样长时间睡眠——可以说，醒来只是为了活动身体、获取食物，后来才为了交配。那确实是一个如梦似幻的世界，却充满迷人活力，梦境中的想象力狂野地嬉戏于这个新冒险所蕴含的所有可能性中：构想各种可能的语言与沟通形式，编织关于未来文明的宏大梦境故事，这些故事自带完整的历史——因为他们已与时间结盟，所以能构建出自动创造过去与未来的心灵殿堂。

quotes.text: "They were these: that the entire world with its organization was kept together by certain stories, like those of the Roman Catholic Church; that it was dangerous beyond all knowing to look through the stories or examine them for the truth, and that all kinds of taboos existed to keep us from doing this, since ... on the other side, so to speak, there was an incomprehensible frightening chaotic dimension, malevolent; powers beyond our imagining; and that to question the stories was to threaten not just personal survival but the fabric of reality as we know it. So excommunication was the punishment, or damnation ... which meant more than mere ostracism, but the complete isolation of a person from those belief systems, with nothing between him or her and those frightening realities ... without a framework in which to even organize meaning. This was what damnation really meant. To seek truth was the most dangerous of well-intentioned behavior, then ... and retribution had to be swift and sure.
quotes.translation: "这些信念包括：整个有组织的世界是靠某些故事维系，比如罗马天主教会的故事；透过这些故事审视或探究真相是极其危险的，各种禁忌的存在就是为了阻止我们这样做，因为……可以说，在故事的另一面，存在着难以理解的可怕混沌维度，充满恶意；那是超越我们想象的力量；质疑故事不仅威胁个人生存，更威胁我们所知的现实结构。因此惩罚就是逐出教会或永罚……这不仅意味着排斥，更意味着将一个人完全隔绝于那些信仰体系之外，让他或她直面那些可怕现实而毫无屏障……甚至没有一个框架来组织意义。这才是永罚的真正含义。在当时，追求真理是最危险的善意行为……而惩罚必须迅速且确定。

quotes.text: A certain portion of physical growth, in terms of a series of physical moments, is therefore necessary for value fulfillment to show itself within a physical organism. Within the dream field and within many other systems, this series of moments is unknown. Development comes not from a series of actions strung out along a single line, one before the other in lengthwise fashion. Instead development is largely a matter of value fulfillment, which is achieved through the perspectives of action, through traveling within any given action, and following it and changing with it. To make this clearer, I have said that action exists within limitless perspectives, and that you are mainly familiar with it as it is materialized along a single line of continuity within the physical system. You experience action then as if you were moving along a single line, each dot on the line representing a moment of your time. But at the imaginary point on your line that represents any given moment, action moves out in all directions. From the standpoint of that moment point, you could imagine action forming an imaginary circle with that point as an apex. But this happens at the point of every moment.
quotes.translation: 因此，就一系列物理瞬间而言，为了价值实现能在物理有机体中显现，一定程度的物理成长是必要的。在梦境领域和许多其他系统中，这种瞬间序列并不存在。发展并非来自沿单一线条纵向排列的一系列行动。相反，发展主要是价值实现的问题，通过行动的视角、通过在任何给定行动中穿行、跟随并与之变化来实现。为了更清楚说明，我曾说过行动存在于无限视角中，而你们主要熟悉的是它在物理系统中沿单一线条具体化的形式。因此你们体验行动时，仿佛沿着一条线移动，线上的每个点代表你们时间的一个瞬间。但在你们线上代表任何给定瞬间的想象点上，行动会向所有方向延伸。从那个瞬间点的立场来看，你们可以想象行动形成一个以该点为顶点的想象圆。但这种情况在每个瞬间点都会发生。

quotes.text: Now Ruburt's energies are being released, and once this process begins, as it has, then all the inner improvement will seem to appear at once, though they are the results of several months. The psychic work will pick up, and indeed already has. The desire shown in your personal life on Ruburt's part is very important. Ruburt should tell himself now that all other tensions can leave through his fingertips, not back up there you see but go completely outward. It will help if he imagines tension streaming outward through the fingertips. His idea to have long hair (smile) at this particular point is beneficial, for it means to him the luxury of both sexuality and sense, sensual extravagance. These qualities directly in opposition to the overly restrictive, puritan rigidities that he was in the past heir to.
quotes.translation: 现在鲁柏的能量正在释放，一旦这个过程开始（正如已经发生的），所有内在改善似乎会立刻显现，尽管它们是几个月努力的结果。灵性工作将加速，事实上已经如此。鲁柏在个人生活中表现出的渴望非常重要。鲁柏现在应该告诉自己，所有其他紧张都可以通过指尖离开，不是回流到那里，而是完全向外释放。如果他想象紧张感通过指尖向外流淌，会很有帮助。在这个特殊时期他留长发的想法（微笑）是有益的，因为对他而言这象征着性感和感官的奢华放纵。这些品质直接与他过去继承的过度拘谨、清教式的刻板形成对比。

quotes.text: You must mentally wipe out the negative image, for example. If you think that tomorrow Johnny F will misbehave in study hall, you should, in your mind, replace this with the image of Johnny F behaving very well.
