quotes.text: The personality itself exists in many dimensions, as you know, and it has its reality within many other fields than the physical field, and is indeed basically not nearly as allied with the physical field as you may imagine. These unifying principles of which I spoke are themselves main, dominating groups of actions, about which the main energies of the personality group themselves.
quotes.translation: 人格本身存在于许多维度中，如你所知，它在物理领域之外的许多其他领域中也具有其实相，并且实际上与物理领域的关联远不如你可能想象的那么紧密。我提到的这些统一原则本身就是主导性的行动群组，人格的主要能量围绕它们聚集。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: You seem to think that there is an expanse of time between reincarnational existences, that one follows the other as one moment seems to follow another. Because you perceive a reality of cause and effect, you hypothesize a reality in which one life affects the next one. With your theories of guilt and punishment you often imagine that you are hampered in this existence by guilts collected in the last life -- or worse, accumulated through the centuries.
quotes.translation: 你似乎认为转世存在之间有一段漫长的时间间隔，一个接一个地发生，就像瞬间似乎一个接一个地到来。因为你感知到因果实相，你假设了一个实相，其中一生影响下一生。凭借你的罪与罚理论，你常常想象自己在这一生中受到上一生积累的罪过所阻碍——或者更糟，是几个世纪以来累积的罪过。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You must work from your own subjective experience, so when you find your own finest focus point, that is your clearest reception for your own home station. You may feel that it has a certain position in your inner vision, or in your head, or you may find that you have your own symbol to represent it. You might imagine it, if you want to, as a station indicator on your own radio or television set, but your subjective recognition of it is your own cue.
quotes.translation: 你必须从自己的主观经验出发，所以当你找到自己最精微的焦点时，那就是你对自身本台最清晰的接收点。你可能会觉得它在你的内在视觉或头部有特定位置，或者你可能会发现有自己的符号来代表它。如果你愿意，你可以把它想象成自己收音机或电视机上的电台指示器，但你对它的主观认知才是你自己的线索。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Each particular "station" of consciousness perceives in a different kind of reality, and as mentioned earlier (in Session 711, for instance), you usually tune in to your home station most of the time. If you turn your focus only slightly away, the world appears differently; and if that slightly altered focus were the predominant one, then that is how the world would seem to be. Each aspect of the psyche perceives the reality upon which it is focused, and that reality is also the materialization of a particular state of the psyche projected outward. You can learn to encounter other realities by altering your position within your own psyche.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: After you have managed to feel yourself above your body in your astral form, you again tell yourself to see the image of the same past self, you may then imagine its image merging with your own, and its memories a part of your own consciousness. When you have succeeded, then still feeling yourself in the astral form, tell yourself that you will next see the image of the life before last: the image of the person you were, then repeat the other steps.
quotes.translation: 在你设法以星光体形态感觉到自己高于身体之后，再次告诉自己看到同一过去自我的影像，你可能会想象它的影像与你的融合，它的记忆成为你意识的一部分。当你成功时，仍然感觉自己在星光体形态中，告诉自己接下来会看到上一世之前的影像：你曾经是那个人的影像，然后重复其他步骤。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Not one portion of consciousness is lost in this process, you understand. When you think of a possible destruction of your planet, you imagine perhaps the mutilation of nature as you know it, the species changed. Or you imagine the race of man vanishing, or starting all over again as it were from scratch.
quotes.translation: 在这个过程中，没有一丝意识会丢失，你明白吗。当你想到自己星球可能的毁灭时，你可能会想象你所知的自然被肢解，物种改变。或者你想象人类种族消失，或者仿佛从头开始一切。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Pretend a particular event happened that greatly disturbed you. In your mind imagine it not simply wiped out, but replaced by another event of more beneficial nature. Now this must be done with great vividness and emotional validity, and many times. It is not a self-deception. The event that you choose will automatically be a probable event, which did in fact happen, though it is not the event you chose to perceive in your given probable past.
quotes.translation: 假装某件让你极度不安的特定事件发生了。在你的脑海中想象它不仅仅被抹去，而是被另一个更有益性质的事件所取代。这必须以极大的生动性和情感有效性进行，并且多次重复。这不是自我欺骗。你选择的事件自动会是一个可能事件，它实际上确实发生了，尽管这不是你选择在给定的可能过去中感知到的事件。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now the evil which Ruburt imagined he was projecting outward does not exist, but because he believed it did, he formed his materialization from his fears. It was the shape of the desolation he had felt last weekend. Now in larger terms, and in the deepest sense, there is no evil, only your lack of perception, but I know this is difficult for you to accept. But this fact is Ruburt's safeguard in his astral travels--as long as he remembers it.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏想象中向外投射的邪恶并不存在，但因他相信其存在，便从恐惧中形成了自己的物质化。那是他上周末感受到的荒芜形态。从更宏观的层面和最深层意义而言，邪恶并不存在，只有你们感知的匮乏——但我知道这对你们而言难以接受。然而这个事实正是鲁柏在星光体旅行中的保障——只要他记得这一点。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The invisible barriers of which I have spoken hold you back, so that when your perception fails to yield results then you imagine that there is nothing to perceive. Therefore you imagine that you perceive the whole of an atom, but you only perceive that portion which is projected into your own system.
quotes.translation: 我曾提及的无形屏障阻碍着你们，当你们的感知无法获得结果时，便想象没有可感知之物。因此你们自以为感知到了原子的全貌，实则只感知到投射入自身系统的那部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: This is extremely difficult, I should imagine. Nevertheless, even when an inner self has sent out a new outer ego upon a new camouflage venture, the previous ego is still afforded an almost unlimited avenue for development. There are many possibilities for it. It can choose to remain what it is, one ego. It will then remain in a somewhat subordinate position to the inner self, but in no more of a subordinate position than it was earlier.
quotes.translation: 这想必极为艰深。然而当内在自体将新的外在自我派往新的伪装冒险时，先前的自我仍拥有近乎无限的发展途径。它存在诸多可能性：可以选择维持原状，作为一个自我存在。届时它将处于较内在自体略为从属的位置，但绝不比先前更卑微。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Despite this, I'm aware of the fact that I was necessary to the production of Seth's book. He needs my ability with words; even, I think, my turn of mind. Certainly my writing training aids in the translation of his material and helps give it form, no matter how unconsciously this is done. Certain personality characteristics are important too, I imagine -- the agility with which I can switch the focus of my consciousness, for example.
quotes.translation: 尽管如此，我深知自己对赛斯书的诞生不可或缺。他需要我的语言能力，甚至包括我的思维方式。我的写作训练无疑有助于转化他的资料并赋予其形式——无论这过程多么无意识。某些人格特质也至关重要，比如我转换意识焦点的敏捷度。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth began dictating the book in our next Session 511, January 21, 1970, and finished it in Session 591, August 11, 1971. The intervening sessions did not all involve book dictation, however. Some were devoted to personal matters, some given for specific people who needed help, and some were in answer to philosophical questions not connected with the book. I also took several "little vacations." Despite such layoffs, Seth always picked right up, precisely where he left off dictation.

quotes.text: When children dream, they utilize these inner senses as adults do, and then through dreaming they learn to translate such material into the precise framework of the exterior senses. Children's games are always "in the present" -- that is, they are immediately experienced, though the play events may involve the future or the past. The phrase "once upon a time" is strongly evocative and moving, even to adults, because children play with time in a way that adults have forgotten. If you want to sense the motion of your psyche, it is perhaps easiest to imagine a situation either in the past or the future, for this automatically moves your mental sense-perceptions in a new way.
quotes.translation: 孩童做梦时与成人一样运用内在感官，通过梦境学会将此类资料转化为外在感官的精确框架。孩童的游戏永远"存在于当下"——即即时体验，即便游戏事件可能涉及未来或过去。"很久很久以前"这个短语对成人也具有强烈感染力，因为孩童以成人遗忘的方式玩弄时间。若想感知心灵的运动，最简便的方式或许是想象过去或未来的某个情境，这能自动以全新方式驱动你的心智感知。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It is energy concentrated to a degree quite unbelievable to you. It contains potentials unlimited, but it must work out its own identity and form its own worlds. It carries within it the burden of all being. Within it are personality potentials beyond your comprehension. Remember, this is your own soul or entity I am speaking of, as well as soul or entity in general. You are one manifestation of your own soul. How many of you would want to limit your reality, your entire reality, to the experience you now know? You do this when you imagine that your present self is your entire personality, or insist that your identity be maintained unchanged through an endless eternity.
quotes.translation: 这是你们难以想象的极度凝聚的能量。它蕴含无限潜能，却必须发展自身特性并构建自己的世界。它承载着所有存在的重负，内蕴超越你们理解的人格潜能。请记住，我谈论的正是你们自己的灵魂或存有，也是广义上的灵魂或存有。你们是自身灵魂的一种显化。有多少人愿意将自身实相——全部实相——局限于此刻已知的经验？当你们想象当前自我就是完整人格，或坚持身份必须永恒不变时，正是在做这样的事。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In some systems colors are used as a prime method of communication. They are sent out telepathically, each gradation of such variety that you cannot now imagine it. These intricate color communications follow the almost endless emotional shadings possible. It is difficult you see to verbalize this concept. They have to begin with more spectrums than those with which you are familiar. They live in an entirely different senseuniverse.
quotes.translation: 在某些系统中，颜色被用作主要的沟通方式。它们以心电感应方式传递，每一种色调的细微变化之丰富，远超你目前的想象。这些精微的色彩沟通追随几乎无穷无尽的情感层次。你看，要将这个概念用语言表达出来是很困难的。他们必须从比你所熟悉的更多光谱开始。他们生活在一个完全不同的感官宇宙中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: In daily language, objects have certain names. Obviously the names are not the objects, but symbols for them. Even these symbols, however, divide you as the perceiver from the rest of the world, which becomes objectified. You can yourself understand far more about the nature of the psyche, for example, than you think that you can. To do this, however, you must leave your daily language behind at least momentarily, and pay attention to your own feelings and imagination. Your language tells you that certain things are true, or facts, and that certain things are not. Many of your most vivid and moving feelings do not fit the facts of your language, so you disregard them.
quotes.translation: 在日常语言中，物体有特定的名称。显然，名称并非物体本身，而是它们的象征符号。然而，即便是这些象征符号，也将你作为感知者与其余世界分隔开来，使世界被客体化。例如，你对自己心灵本质的了解，远比你自认为的要多。但要达成这一点，你必须至少暂时抛开日常语言，转而关注自己的感受与想象力。你的语言告诉你某些事情是真实的、是事实，而某些事情则不然。但你许多最生动、最动人的感受并不符合你语言中的"事实"，于是你便忽略了它们。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The concepts of God that you have, have gone hand-in-hand with the development of your consciousness. The ego, emerging, needed to feel its dominance and control, and so it imagined a dominant god apart from nature. Often nations acted as group egos -- each with its own god-picturing, its own concepts of power. Whenever a tribe or a group or a nation decided to embark upon a war, it always used the concept of its god to lead it on.
quotes.translation: 你所拥有的上帝概念，与你的意识发展是相辅相成的。自我在浮现时，需要感受其支配与控制，因此它想象出一个独立于自然之外的、具有支配性的神。国家常常扮演着群体自我的角色——每个国家都有自己描绘的神祇形象，以及自己对权力的概念。每当一个部落、群体或国家决定发动战争时，它总是利用其神的概念来引领前行。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: In your terms, until now your consciousness has specialized in neurological patterning. As mentioned (in Session 682), this was extremely important while it learned the art of specialized focus. Now, however, it must begin to recognize that it can indeed expand, and bring into its awareness other quite legitimate realities. The nature of probabilities must be understood, for the time has come in the world as you experience it where the greatest wisdom and discrimination are needed. Your consciousness and neurological prejudice blind you to the full dimension of physical activity. The true implications of physical action are not as yet apparent to you.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Our books are in the regular trade department. This poses some problems for the legal department, which is given to the most literal translation of reality as interpreted through law. You have almost what you could call a schizophrenic relationship, existing, say, between Parker Books and Prentice's trade-book division. The textbook division represents the workings of the intellect in the usual terms of rational thought, and in those books the qualities of the imagination, of the psyche, of poetry, of creativity, are quite lacking. Such qualities are indeed considered threats, for they do not accept easy answers, and are not content with the status quo.
quotes.translation: 我们的书籍属于常规的贸易部门。这给法律部门带来了一些问题，因为他们倾向于对现实进行最字面的翻译，即通过法律来诠释现实。你们几乎可以说，在帕克图书公司与普伦蒂斯贸易图书部门之间存在着一种类似精神分裂的关系。教科书部门代表的是理性思维常规术语下的智力运作，而在那些书籍中，想象力、心灵、诗歌与创造力的特质相当匮乏。这些特质确实被视为威胁，因为它们不接受简单的答案，也不满足于现状。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: Nevertheless to me the walls are transparent. So are the wires that we constructed to make our point about fifth dimension, but for all practical purposes we must behave as if the wires are there. There are certain planes which I cannot glimpse from my viewpoint, although I have greater understanding of these things than you. I realize that the changes that must occur before I can view those other planes will occur in me, not in the other planes. Again if you will consider our maze of wires, I will ask you to imagine them filling up everything that is, with your plane and my plane like two small bird's nests in the netlike fabric of some gigantic tree.
quotes.translation: 然而对我来说，墙壁是透明的。我们为论证第五维度而搭建的那些线也是如此，但出于所有实际目的，我们必须表现得好像那些线确实存在。从我所在的层面，有些层面是我无法瞥见的，尽管我对这些事物的理解比你更深。我意识到，在我能够看见那些其他层面之前，必须发生的变化是在我之内，而非在其他层面。如果你再次考虑我们那迷宫般的线网，我会请你想象它们充满了存在的一切，而你的层面与我的层面就像巨大树状网络结构中的两个小鸟巢。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: It is possible to pick the places you will visit. Whether or not you can achieve such purposeful visits at this time, I do not know. However, you can most certainly try. You are further along than even I had imagined, and so it is possible that such purposeful visits are within your abilities now. At all such occasions, try to establish some kind of contact if possible, but do not be discouraged if this is not achieved at once.
quotes.translation: 你可以选择你要探访的地点。至于你目前能否实现这种有目的的探访，我不知道。然而，你绝对可以尝试。你甚至比我原先想象的走得更远，因此这种有目的的探访或许已在你的能力范围之内。在所有这样的场合，如果可能，请尽量建立某种联系，但如果未能立即达成，也不要气馁。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: It is theoretically possible, for example, for any of you to disperse your consciousness and become a part of any object in the room -- or to fly apart, to disperse yourself out into space -- without leaving your sense of identity. This is not practical in your terms, yet many of you do it to gain refreshment while you are sleeping. Consciousness by its very characteristics carries the burden of perception. This is the kind of consciousness you are used to thinking of. You cannot imagine it without perception in your terms; and yet consciousness can be vital and alive without your idea of perception. The last part of that sentence is important.
quotes.translation: 理论上，你们任何人都可能分散自己的意识，成为房间里任何物体的一部分——或者飞散开来，将自己散布到太空中——同时不失去你的身份感。这在你们的说法中并不实际，但你们许多人在睡眠时确实这样做以恢复精神。意识就其本质而言承载着感知的重担。这是你们习惯思考的那种意识。在你们的说法中，你们无法想象没有感知的意识；然而，意识可以在没有你们所认为的感知的情况下保持活力和生机。那句话的最后一部分很重要。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This book had no chapters [in order] to further disrupt your accepted notions of what a book should be. There are different kinds of organizations present, however, and in any given section of the book, several levels of consciousness are appealed to at once. (Intently:) The threads of the work are interwoven so that various portions of your consciousness are sent out, so to speak, on separate journeys of thought and imagination. Yet these side trips are also related. They intertwine, not only through the psychic organization that I have given to "Unknown" Reality, but because of the great uniting nature within the consciousness of each reader.
quotes.translation: 这本书没有章节（按顺序），以进一步打破你们对一本书应该是什么样子的既定观念。然而，书中存在着不同种类的组织，在书的任何特定部分，都会同时诉诸几个层次的意识。（专注地：）作品的线索交织在一起，因此你们意识的不同部分，可以说，被派往思想和想象的不同旅程。然而，这些旁支旅程也是相关的。它们相互交织，不仅通过我给《未知的实相》的心理组织，还因为每位读者意识内部伟大的统一本质。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 743
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: There are literally infinite numbers of sequences, faultlessly activated, that make the existence of your own world possible. I admit that it is sometimes inconceivable to me that a human being can imagine his world to be meaningless, for the very existence of one human body speaks of an almost unbelievable molecular and cellular cooperation that could hardly result through the bounty of the most auspicious works of chance.
quotes.translation: 实际上，有无数个序列，完美无缺地激活，使你们自己世界的存在成为可能。我承认，有时我难以理解，一个人竟然能想象他的世界毫无意义，因为仅仅一个人类身体的存在，就说明了几乎令人难以置信的分子和细胞合作，这几乎不可能通过最幸运的偶然事件的结果而产生。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This sort of experience will always be shielded from the outer ego of necessity. Even a watered-down version of a direct inner experience is a shock to the outer ego on your plane, since the ego imagines itself and its own perceptions to be supreme. You have no idea, even with what training you have, of how shattering such a complete experience would be to the outer ego, so we will take one inner-sense experience at a time.
quotes.translation: 这种体验将永远被必要地屏蔽在外在自我之外。即使是一个稀释过的直接内在体验版本，对你们层面上的外在自我来说也是一种冲击，因为自我想象自己和自己的感知是至高无上的。你们不知道，即使以你们所受的训练，这样一个完整的体验对外在自我来说会是多么具有毁灭性，所以我们将一次只进行一个内在感官体验。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Hypnotism is a safer method to begin with than drugs, but hypnotism itself is not necessary if there is an inner willingness to explore the inner universe. The main barrier is the ego's fear of being subjugated, even as it fears subjugation imagined in death. If the ego can be made to understand that inner awareness, exploration and investigation will actually enlarge its scope of awareness there will be little difficulty.
quotes.translation: 催眠是比药物更安全的开始方法，但如果内心愿意探索内在宇宙，催眠本身并非必要。主要的障碍是自我对被征服的恐惧，就像它害怕在死亡中想象出的被征服一样。如果能让自我理解，内在的觉知、探索和调查实际上会扩大其觉知的范围，那么几乎不会有困难。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: These fields or planes are more various than you can imagine, and they are formed in the same manner, basically, as your own universe was formed, and many of those inhabitants tune in on your universe spontaneously and quite accidentally. But the inhabitants of most such planes are no more aware of your actual existence than you are of their existence.
quotes.translation: 这些场域或层面比你们能想象的更加多样化，它们基本上以与你们自己的宇宙相同的方式形成，许多那些居民自发且相当偶然地调谐到你们的宇宙。但大多数这类层面的居民对你们实际存在的了解，并不比你们对他们的存在的了解多。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Theoretically, a thoroughly educated space traveler in your time, landing upon a strange planet, would be able to adjust his own consciousness so that he could perceive the planet in various "sequences" of time. If you land upon a planet in a spaceship and find volcanoes, you would, perhaps, realize that other portions of that planet might show different faces. You have confidence in your ability to move through space, so you might then explore the terrain that you could not see from your original landing point. If you did not understand the change in qualities of space, you might imagine that the whole planet was a giant volcano.
quotes.translation: 理论上，你们时代一位受过充分教育的太空旅行者，降落在陌生星球上时，能够调整自己的意识，从而在时间的各种"序列"中感知该星球。如果你乘飞船降落在一个星球上并发现火山，你或许会意识到该星球的其他部分可能呈现不同面貌。你对自己穿越空间的能力有信心，于是可能会探索从最初降落点无法看到的地形。如果你不了解空间性质的变化，你可能会想象整个星球都是巨大的火山。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: You think in terms of linear time, and the best you can do to imagine your deeper reality is to consider reincarnation in time. It is a matter of focus. You usually identify with the outside of yourself, and with the outside of the world. You do not, for example, usually identify with the inside of your body, with its organs, much less its cells or atoms -- yet in that direction lies a certain kind of infinity (intently).
quotes.translation: 你们以线性时间思考，而想象自己更深层实相的最佳方式，就是考虑时间中的转世。这是焦点的问题。你通常认同于自己的外在，以及世界的外在。例如，你通常不会认同于自己身体的内在，认同于它的器官，更不用说细胞或原子——然而在那个方向存在着某种无限性（专注地）。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: If you have chosen a familiar destination, then imagine the houses you might pass. It is sometimes easier to choose an unfamiliar location, however, for then you are not tempted to test yourself as you go along by wondering whether or not the imagined scenes conform to your memory.
quotes.translation: 如果你选择了熟悉的目的地，那么就想象你可能经过的房屋。不过，有时选择不熟悉的地点更容易，因为那样你就不会在行进中因想知道想象的场景是否符合记忆而考验自己。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The mother could not consciously control the bodily processes that lead to birth. In the truest sense, the birth magically happens, as miraculous in those terms as the so-called initial emergence of life upon the planet itself. Scientific analysis of the brain will tell you nothing about the power that moves your thoughts, or hint at the source of the brain's abilities. However, the constant activity between Frameworks 1 and 2 is constantly apparent in the very existence of your world, and in the relationships involving your imagination, feelings, and beliefs, and those private and shared events that compose your experience.
quotes.translation: 母亲无法有意识地控制导致分娩的身体过程。从最真实的意义上说，分娩是神奇地发生的，就像所谓的生命最初出现在这颗星球上一样神奇。对大脑的科学分析不会告诉你是什么力量驱动你的思想，也不会暗示大脑能力的来源。然而，架构一与架构二之间的持续活动，在你世界的存在本身中，以及在你想象、感受、信念之间的关系中，在构成你经验的私密与共享事件中，始终显而易见。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There have been guides to help her. She will hardly notice that she has entirely moved in, for she will feel so at home. In her case, she has been forming memory pictures of her childhood, and entering into them, and of days before the physical illness, and she has had help in these endeavors. She is learning that events that seem to be in the past can be recreated. She does not imagine that she is a child. She is enjoying the freedom of revisiting and re-experiencing certain events. This is also a sort of spiritual therapy in her case, so that she loses the identification with illness, and does not carry it with her.
quotes.translation: 一直有向导在帮助她。她几乎不会注意到自己已经完全搬进去了，因为她会感到如此自在。就她而言，她一直在形成童年记忆的画面，并进入其中，还有身体疾病之前的日子，她在这些努力中得到了帮助。她正在学习，看似属于过去的事件可以被重新创造。她并不想象自己是个孩子。她正在享受重访和重新体验某些事件的自由。就她的情况而言，这也是一种灵性疗法，使她摆脱对疾病的认同，不将其带在身边。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Now I do not mean this to be merely a symbolic exercise, for though it may begin with imagination, it is based upon fact, and emanations from your consciousness and the creativity of your soul do indeed reach outward in that manner. The exercise will give you some idea of the true nature, creativity, and vitality of the soul from which you can draw your own energy and of which you are an individual and unique portion.
quotes.translation: 现在，我并非意指这仅仅是象征性的练习，因为虽然它可能始于想象，但它是基于事实的，来自你意识的散发和你灵魂的创造力确实以那种方式向外延伸。这个练习会让你对灵魂的真实本质、创造力和生命力有所了解，你可以从中汲取自己的能量，而你是灵魂的一个独特部分。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You must realize, and tell yourself, that creativity is timeless in a basic manner. You must completely cease inner speculations and regrets and questions as to why your abilities have not come to fruition. This must positively be done. The negative suggestion works constantly against you. When such thoughts come to mind, instead tell yourself that that trend of thought will not help your painting, but hinder it, and that it is constricting. Then immediately imagine a time when you painted very well and spontaneously, and tell yourself that you are now free to use and develop these abilities. (Pause.)
quotes.translation: 你必须明白并告诉自己，创造力在基本层面上是超越时间的。你必须完全停止内心的揣测、遗憾和质疑，为什么你的能力未能实现。这必须坚决做到。负面暗示不断对你产生不利影响。当这类念头出现时，反而要告诉自己，这种思维模式不会有助于你的绘画，反而会阻碍它，并且是限制性的。然后立刻想象一个你画得非常好且自发的时刻，并告诉自己，你现在可以自由地运用和发展这些能力。（停顿）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: In play, particularly, children try on any conceivable situation for size. In the dream state adults and children alike do the same thing, and many dreams are indeed a kind of play. The brain itself is never satisfied with one version of an event, but will always use the imagination to form other versions in an activity quite as spontaneous as play. It also practices forming events as the muscles practice motion.
quotes.translation: 尤其在游戏中，孩子们会尝试任何可以想象的情境。在梦境状态中，成人和孩子同样如此，许多梦确实是一种游戏。大脑本身从不满足于对事件的单一版本，而总是会运用想象力形成其他版本，其活动之自发，正如游戏一般。它也在练习形成事件，就像肌肉练习动作一样。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There is no other valid way of changing physical events. It might help if you imagine an inner living dimension within yourself in which you create, in miniature psychic form, all the exterior conditions that you know. Simply put, you do exactly this. Your thoughts, feelings and mental pictures can be called incipient exterior events, for in one way or another each of these is materialized into physical reality.
quotes.translation: 没有其他有效的方式可以改变物质事件。如果你想象自己内在有一个活生生的维度，在其中你以微小的心灵形式创造你所知的所有外在条件，这或许会有帮助。简单来说，你确实正是这样做的。你的思想、情感和心灵画面可以被称为初期的外在事件，因为每一种都以某种方式被物质化为物质实相。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Chemicals themselves will simply not give rise to consciousness or to life. Your scientists will simply have to face the facts that consciousness comes first, and evolves its own form. But this is, and involves, individuality, and also interdependence. The physical body that you imagine consists of some sort of separate consciousness, controlling a framework of completely unconscious parts, is quite farfetched.
quotes.translation: 化学物质本身根本无法产生意识或生命。你的科学家们将不得不面对事实：意识是第一位的，并演化出它自己的形态。但这涉及个体性，也涉及相互依存。你想象的身体由某种独立的意识构成，控制着一个完全无意识部分的框架，这种想法相当牵强。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: In a manner of speaking, your universe and all others spring from a dimension that is the creative source for all realities--a basic dream universe, so to speak, a divine psychological bed where subjective being is sparked, illuminated, stimulated, pierced, by its own infinite desire for creativity. The source of its power is so great that its imaginings become worlds, but it is endowed with a creativity of such splendor that it seeks the finest fulfillment, for even the smallest of its thoughts and all of its potentials are directed with a good intent that is literally beyond all imagining.
quotes.translation: 可以这么说，你的宇宙和所有其他宇宙都源自一个维度，它是所有实相的创造之源——可以说是一个基本的梦境宇宙，一个神圣的心灵之床，在那里主观存在被其自身对创造力的无限渴望所点燃、照亮、激发、穿透。它力量的源头如此巨大，以至于它的想象变成了世界，但它被赋予了如此辉煌的创造力，以至于它寻求最完美的实现，因为它最微小的思想和所有潜能都带着一种善意被引导，这种善意简直超乎一切想象。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The Fox experiments are quite valid, with some changes that you will make, I imagine. At one level of the dreaming state it is possible for you to recognize and align yourself with that portion of yourself that is aware of itself within fourth-dimensional reality. You have both occasionally learned to take our waking consciousness into the dream state, and here Fox is correct, for you must start at this point.
quotes.translation: 狐狸实验相当有效，我想你会做一些调整。在梦境的一个层次上，你可以认出并调整自己，与那部分在第四维度实相中意识到自身的自己对齐。你们俩偶尔都学会了将我们清醒的意识带入梦境状态，而狐狸在这里是对的，因为你必须从这一点开始。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: To some extent, we have discussed your body and its composition of cells (in the 632nd session in Chapter Seven, for instance). All of the cells that now make up your physical form obviously exist at once. Imagine that you have many lives enduring in the same fashion. Instead of cells then you have selves. I told you that each cell has its own memory. The self-memory is, of course, of far greater dimension.
quotes.translation: 在某种程度上，我们已经讨论过你的身体及其细胞组成（例如，在第七章第632节中）。如今构成你肉身的全部细胞显然同时存在。想象你有许多生命以同样方式延续。那时你拥有的不是细胞，而是自我。我告诉过你，每个细胞都有其记忆。而自我的记忆，其维度自然要广阔得多。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: It is literally impossible for you, and it is a logistic contradiction to imagine, with your physical structures, that any perception can be received unless the perceiver's own inner situation is altered. I am trying to make it as clear as possible that information automatically blends with, is intermingled with, and enmeshed with, the entire (Pause), physically-valid (hyphen) structure of the personality.
quotes.translation: 对你而言，以你的肉体结构，要想象任何感知能被接收而感知者自身内在情境不发生改变，这在逻辑上是矛盾的。我正试图尽可能清晰地说明：信息会自动与整个人格的（停顿）肉体有效（连字符）结构相融合、交织、纠缠。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Each layer, or each area of the whole self, imagines that it is the center of awareness, and that the whole self revolves about it. So far you have had little experience with these other "centers"--you may put that in quotation marks--but before too long we shall give you some ideas so that you may become at least slightly acquainted. Initially this will be done through suggestion on a subconscious level.
quotes.translation: 整体自我的每一层或每一区域都想象自己是觉知的中心，而整体自我围绕它旋转。迄今为止，你对这些其他"中心"——你可以加上引号——几乎毫无经验，但不久后我们将给你一些概念，让你至少能稍有了解。最初这将通过潜意识层面的暗示来实现。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Consciousness is a way of perceiving the various dimensions of reality. Consciousness as you know it is highly specialized. The physical senses allow you to perceive the three-dimensional world, and yet by their very nature they can inhibit the perception of other equally valid dimensions. Most of you identify with your daily physically oriented self. You would not think of identifying with one portion of your body and ignoring all other parts, and yet you are doing the same thing (smile) when you imagine that the egotistical self carries the burden of your identity.
quotes.translation: 意识是感知实相不同维度的一种方式。你所知的意识是高度专门化的。肉体感官让你能感知三维世界，然而正因其本质，它们也会抑制对其他同样有效维度的感知。你们大多数人认同于日常以肉体为导向的自我。你不会想到只认同身体的一部分而忽略其他部分，但当你想象自我意识背负着你身份的负担时，你正在做同样的事（微笑）。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The theory of evolution,4 for instance, is an imaginative construct, and yet through its lights some generations now have viewed their world. It is not only that you think of yourselves differently, but you actually experience a different kind of self. Your institutions change their aspects accordingly, so that experience fits the beliefs that you have about it. You act in certain ways. You view the entire universe in a fashion that did not exist before, so that imagination and belief intangibly structure your subjective experience and your objective circumstances.
quotes.translation: 例如，进化论是一种想象力的建构，然而通过它的光芒，几代人已经这样看待他们的世界。你不仅以不同方式思考自己，而且实际上体验着一种不同的自我。你的机构相应地改变其面貌，使经验符合你对此所持的信念。你以特定方式行动。你以一种前所未有的方式看待整个宇宙，因此想象力和信念无形中构建了你的主观经验和客观环境。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

SESSION 829, MARCH 22, 1978 
9:30 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: What changes do they make? How many of them, after criticizing their elders, look inward into their own hearts? How many demand that they use their own potentials? How many demand the best of themselves? And it is this that you must do. Anything less is disaster. The exploration of inner space--this is your mission. It will lead you into more exciting realms than any you can imagine. It will literally reshape and remake your world. If you see but one item within your universe and it disgusts you, then look within yourself, for you have helped create it.
quotes.translation: 他们做出了什么改变？在批评长辈之后，有多少人向内审视自己的心灵？有多少人要求自己运用自身潜能？有多少人要求自己做到最好？而这就是你必须做的。任何不足都是灾难。探索内在空间——这是你的使命。它将引领你进入比你所能想象的任何领域都更激动人心的领域。它将切实重塑并改造你的世界。如果你在宇宙中看到一样事物令你厌恶，那就向内审视自己，因为你曾协助创造了它。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: The distinctions formed by the outer senses therefore actually limit perception as a whole, while intensifying it into a small but vivid, seemingly enclosed radius of reality. When conceptual thought develops far enough, then it is imagined that all energy originates from what seems to be a closed system; and this misconception then colors all deductions made concerning the nature of energy itself.
quotes.translation: 外在感官所形成的区分实际上限制了整体的感知，同时却将感知强化为一个微小但生动、看似封闭的现实范围。当概念性思维发展到足够程度时，人们便会想象所有能量都源自一个看似封闭的系统；这种误解随后会染上所有关于能量本质本身的推论。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Imagine looking at a scene outside your window as Ruburt now allows me to do. From Ruburt's viewpoint he receives the visual image with auditory effects. A slightly cold draft of air leaks in through the window. He does not smell anything from the outside. With use of the complete set of inner senses the experience would be to you astonishingly more rich, varied, direct, and instantly instructive.
quotes.translation: 想象一下，就像鲁柏现在允许我这样做那样，看着窗外的景象。从鲁柏的视角，他接收到带有听觉效果的视觉影像。一丝微凉的冷风从窗户缝隙渗入。他闻不到任何来自外界的气味。若运用全套内在感官，这体验对你而言将会惊人地更丰富、更多样、更直接，且即刻具有启发性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: If however you imagine that the environment or physical condition is the reality, then you can feel trapped by it, and spend your efforts fighting a paper dragon. The environment is always altered from the inside. There is instant feedback between the interior and exterior conditions, but the mobility, the necessity and the method of changing the physical environment will always come from within.
quotes.translation: 然而，如果你想象环境或物质状况就是实相，那么你会感到被它困住，并将精力耗费在与纸老虎搏斗上。环境总是从内在被改变。内在与外在状况之间存在即时反馈，但改变物质环境的机动性、必要性及方法，永远来自内在。

quotes.text: The impulse to be, in any terms that you can understand, is without beginning or end. What you have in your physical species are the manifestations of inner species of being, or creative groupings originated by consciousness as material patterns into which consciousness then flows. quotes.text: For an exercise, keep notes for a day or so of all the times you find yourself thinking of probable actions,2 large or small. quotes.text: I have used the phrase "moment of reflection" several times because it is another attribute peculiar to the conscious mind and, again in your terms, is largely denied to the rest of creaturehood. Without that pause -- in which man can remember past in the present, and envisage a future -- natural guilt would have no meaning. Man would not be able to recall past acts, judge them against the present situation, or imagine the future sense of guilt that might result.
quotes.translation: 我多次使用"反思时刻"这个说法，因为这是意识心特有的另一项属性，并且就你们的说法而言，其他生物在很大程度上被剥夺了这种能力。没有这个停顿——人类在其中能于当下回忆过去、展望未来——自然的内疚感将毫无意义。人类将无法回忆过往行为，无法对照当下情境加以评判，也无法想象未来可能产生的内疚感。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The Tubbs woman was to be given still another child, when no more were desired, by Ruburt in childish retaliation. Both literal interpretations were true, you see, but Ruburt took the opportunity （unconsciously of course） to use valid information, that of the trip, as a method of getting back for the imagined and unreasonable treachery of her friend.
quotes.translation: 当不再需要更多孩子时，鲁柏却以孩子气的报复心态，让塔布斯女士又怀上一个孩子。你看，两种字面解释都成立，但鲁柏（当然是无意识地）借机利用那次旅行的有效信息，作为报复她朋友那想象中且毫无道理的背叛的手段。

quotes.text: Let us first consider the trance state. Let us for example consider the following circumstances, which are happening only in our imagination. His focus of attention is rather severely limited in some aspects, and yet in other ways it is very strongly focused.
quotes.translation: 让我们先考虑出神状态。举例来说，让我们设想以下情境——这些情境仅发生在我们的想象中。因此我们将考虑这个想象的情境：一个人处于出神状态。他的注意力焦点在某些方面受到相当严格的限制，但在其他方面却非常集中。

quotes.text: The husband, now, should follow this exercise three times dail: He should imagine the energy and vitality of the universe filling his wife's form with vitality and health. Not a wishful thinking sort of thing, but a definite effort to understand that her form is indeed composed of this energy, and in this way he can help her use it to her advantage.
quotes.translation: 这位丈夫现在应该每天做三次这个练习：他应该想象宇宙的能量与活力充满他妻子的形体，赋予她活力与健康。这不是一厢情愿的幻想，而是明确地努力去理解她的形体确实由这种能量构成，这样他就能帮助她善用这股能量。

quotes.text: "I got a definition of master events but forgot some of it ... to the effect that master events are spectacular events whose main thrusts are outside of time, but whose actions on or in time [are] extravagant--out of proportion to their actual historical connections. The physical part of [such an] event in history is actually minimal in contrast to its effects ... and something about master events touching the worlds of imagination and reason in different ways.
quotes.translation: "我得到了主事件的定义，但忘记了一部分……大意是主事件是壮观的事件，其主要驱动力在时间之外，但其在时间中或时间上的行动却显得夸张——与其实际历史关联不成比例。这类事件在历史上的物质部分，与其影响相比实际上微乎其微……还有关于主事件以不同方式触及想象与理性世界的内容。"

quotes.text: There are other methods also, but they are lesser in importance and not relied upon generally. My method does involve the strong possibility of some distortions and the active cooperation of my students, but this presents a challenge to me rather than a detriment. I imagine that I myself will be the brunt of a few jokes because of tonight's little misfortune.
quotes.translation: 还有其他方法，但重要性较低，通常不被倚重。总有一天我们必须进行一些测试，为了在同僚中保住我的面子，我希望你能顺利过关。我的方法确实存在一些扭曲的可能性，也需要学生们的积极合作，但这对我而言是挑战而非缺陷。我想因为今晚这点小意外，我自己会成为几个笑话的主角。

quotes.text: It is natural of course that you interpret projections from other realities into your own, according to the laws and limitations that seem to apply to your own system. You cannot however understand much concerning even the basic structure of your own universe unless you make some attempt, at least in imagination, to project yourselves beyond it.
quotes.translation: 你自然会根据似乎适用于自身系统的法则与限制，将来自其他实相的信息投射到自己的实相中。然而，除非你至少尝试在想象中超越自身系统，否则连自己宇宙的基本结构都无法真正理解。

quotes.text: There are systems of probability not connected with your own system at all, much more advanced than any you presently imagine, and in these, the truths of which I have been speaking are well known. In them individuals creatively and purposely create realities, knowing how to do so and giving full rein to the creative abilities of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 存在一些与你们系统完全无关的可能性系统，它们比你们目前能想象的任何系统都要先进得多。在这些系统中，我一直在谈论的真理是众所周知的。在这些系统里，个体创造性地、有目的地创造实相，他们知道如何做到这一点，并让意识的创造能力得到充分发挥。

quotes.text: One is that because objects just originate in man's imagination anyway, there's always a strong connection between objects and man's dreams. quotes.translation: 其一，既然物体本就源于人类的想象，那么物体与人类的梦境之间始终存在着紧密的联系。它们作为内在实相的象征，因此无论人类是否意识到，他们感知物体的方式都使得这些物体也代表着最初源于梦境的象征符号。

quotes.text: Imagine where your breath goes when it leaves your body, how it escapes through an open window perhaps and becomes a part of the space outside, where you would never recognize it -- and when it has left you it is no longer a part of what you are, for you are already different. So the lives you have lived are not you, while they are of you.
quotes.translation: 想象你的气息离开身体后的去向，它或许会穿过敞开的窗户，融入你再也无法辨认的外部空间——当它离开你时，便不再是你的一部分，因为此刻的你已经不同。因此，你曾活过的那些人生并非你，但它们却源于你。

quotes.text: In part, whatever disapproval they feel, and it is not nearly as great as you imagine it to be, is based upon jealousy in a large degree, and yet, conversely, you have given them strength, and even helped to justify them, in that underneath it all they do realize that despite their own shortcomings you have managed to achieve something.
quotes.translation: 他们感到的不赞同——其实远没有你想象中那么强烈——很大程度上源于嫉妒；但反过来说，你给了他们力量，甚至帮他们找到了正当性，因为在内心深处他们明白，尽管自身有缺陷，你终究成就了一些事。

quotes.text: The dream solutions are held as the ideals, however. Without for example mass dreams, your United Nations would never exist. This type of mass dream is one of several varieties. quotes.translation: 然而，梦的解决方案被视为理想。例如，没有大众的梦，你们的联合国根本不会存在。这种大众梦是众多梦境类型之一。确实，所有梦在某种程度上都是共享的——正如鲁柏正确推测的那样，你们以为存在于梦中的私密性，其实是一种幻相。

The same sort of error here exists concerning the life force, as I mentioned. You are merged with the life force now, and no one can deny that you are individualistic.
quotes.translation: 但当你离开时间一，或因为死亡而离开时间一时，没有理由想象时间一能与基本时间分离而独立存在。正如我提过的，这里存在关于生命力的同类错误。此刻你正与生命力融合，无人能否认你的个体性。

quotes.text: You are not to hammer at yourself consciously. Imagination and emotion are your great allies. Your conscious direction will automatically bring them into play. quotes.translation: 你不可刻意鞭笞自己。想象与情感是你伟大的盟友。你的意识方向会自动将它们调动起来。由此可知，检视所有关于自我及实相本质的信念何其重要——若你允许，一个信念会引领你走向另一个。

quotes.text: In the life of the psyche a dream is no more or less "true," whether or not it is duplicated in waking life. Dream events happen in a different context -- one, you might say, of the imagination. Here you experience a valid reality that exists on its own, so to speak; one in which the psyche's own language is given greater freedom.
quotes.translation: 在心灵的生命中，一个梦是否在清醒生活中重现，其"真实性"并无差别。梦境事件发生在不同的脉络中——你可以称之为想象的脉络。在此，你体验着一个自成一体的有效实相；在此，心灵自身的语言获得了更大的自由。

quotes.text: If, for example, at two o'clock your apartment appears comfortable, cozy and peaceful and twenty minutes later it suddenly seems crowded, uncomfortable and cluttered, then you see this is not imagination. quotes.translation: 例如，若在两点钟时你的公寓显得舒适、温馨而宁静，二十分钟后却突然变得拥挤、不适且杂乱，那么你便明白这并非想象。你改变了直接物理环境中的关系，并切实感知到了这种变化。

quotes.translation: 你或许会微微一笑，暗自思忖：譬如，想象一位罗马元老院议员对着麦克风向群众演讲，而他的孩子们则在电视上观看他的表演，这实在困难。但这一切极具误导性。进步并不以你所认为的方式存在，正如时间亦非如此。

quotes.text: Self structures and identities are not the same. Identity requires no structure. quotes.translation: 自我结构与身份认同并不相同。身份认同无需结构。身份认同知晓，且知晓自己知晓。自我结构知晓，却不知晓自己知晓。自我结构是身份认同的一部分。身份认同能包含未经转化的纯粹知识，并用以播撒各种存在之种，形成实相。以你们的说法，它们有意识地如此运作。它们比你们目前所能想象的更具意识。

quotes.text: I mentioned (in the 619th session) a game in which you playfully adopt an idea that you want to materialize, then imagine it happening in your mind. Know that all events are mental and psychic first and that these will happen in physical terms, but do not keep watching yourself. Continue with the game.
quotes.translation: 我曾（在第619节）提到一个游戏：你以游戏心态采纳一个想要物质化的想法，然后在脑海中想象它正在发生。要知道所有事件首先都是心智与心灵的，这些事件终将以物质形式显现，但不要持续监视自己。继续这个游戏。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: I am going to tie this in with material dealing with the differences that you seem to see in one particular object. This difference is deeper than you imagine. It is not to be explained by saying that one man sees a given object differently than another because of a particular mood that may assail him.
quotes.translation: 我将把这与探讨特定物体表面差异的材料联系起来。这种差异远比你所想象的更深。不能简单地解释为：由于某人可能受特定情绪侵扰，所以他对同一物体的看法与他人不同。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: The only way out of it is to become aware of your beliefs, aware of your own conscious thought, and to change your beliefs so that you bring them more in line with the kind of reality you want to experience. Imagination and emotion will then automatically come into play to reinforce the new beliefs.
quotes.translation: 唯一的出路是觉察你的信念，觉察你自身的意识思维，并改变你的信念，使其更符合你想要体验的现实类型。想象力与情感随后会自动发挥作用，强化这些新信念。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: It is as if you were sent into some strange and fascinating meadow, and given only the sense of sight. Imagine what you would miss: the odor of the fresh earth, the sounds, the touch of earth beneath your feet, of sun upon your back; using only the sense of smell, you would also be severely limited.
quotes.translation: 这就像你被送入一片奇异迷人的草地，却只被赋予视觉。想象你会错过什么：新鲜泥土的气息、声响、脚下大地的触感、背上阳光的温度；若仅用嗅觉，你同样会受到严重限制。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: They are adopted to meet the particular requirements of the environment in which they are used. In some dreams individuals see what they imagine to he the image of their own dead self. Instead they have been projecting, and see one of their own projected forms that they have themselves just vacated.
quotes.translation: 它们被采用以适应当下环境的具体需求。在某些梦境中，个体看到自己以为是自己死亡形象的影像。实际上他们一直在投射，并看见自己刚刚脱离的某个投射形态。

Because you are space-time oriented, her realizations, accepted momentarily as physical reality, cause gaps in what you think of as normal experience.
quotes.translation: 我说过（第十四章第653节）概率是被实现的……她突然向自己（想象中）的事件敞开，视其为实相。由于你受时空导向，她那些被暂时接纳为物质实相的认知，会在你认为的"正常经验"中造成裂隙。

quotes.text: Your scientists are, generally now, intellectually oriented, believing in reason above the intuitions, taking it for granted that those qualities are opposites. They cannot imagine (pause) life's "initial" creative source, for in their terms it would remind them of creativity's feminine basis.
quotes.translation: 你们的科学家目前普遍偏向理智，相信理性高于直觉，并理所当然地认为这些特质是对立的。他们无法想象（停顿）生命"最初"的创造源头，因为按他们的说法，这会让他们联想到创造力阴性的基础。

quotes.text: The image is tied up with certain emotions usually connected with the institution of marriage, and with being a respectable breadwinner and a father. You have not completely introduced your inner image, but only hinted at it, so that to her it is secondary and unreal, when she is faced by actual events in which you behave as the inner image, she is bewildered, confused, and imagines that you are demented.
quotes.translation: 这个意象与通常和婚姻制度、以及作为体面养家者和父亲相关的某些情感紧密相连。你尚未完全引入你的内在意象，只是暗示了它，以至于对她而言，当面对你行为符合内在意象的实际事件时，这个意象是次要且不真实的，她感到困惑、混乱，并认为你精神错乱了。

quotes.text: You are used to thinking in terms of heredity. In physical terms, and in a different way than you imagine, this is important. Certain earth experiences however are dependent upon duration in time, and result as a consequence of the mind playing upon its experience through long earth seasons.
quotes.translation: 你习惯于从遗传的角度思考。在物质层面，以不同于你想象的方式，这一点很重要。然而，某些地球体验依赖于时间中的持续，并作为心智通过漫长地球季节在其经验上运作的结果而产生。

quotes.text: Without the determination of the inner ego, cohesion of identity would be impossible. The self as you know it is many things, and contains many more vestibules and rooms than you now imagine. Even the outer ego contains multitudinous chambers and interconnections of which you are unaware.
quotes.translation: 没有内在自我的决心，身份的凝聚将不可能。你所知的自我包含许多事物，并拥有比你目前想象的更多的门厅和房间。即使外在自我也包含许多你未曾察觉的众多密室和相互连接。

quotes.text: What is imaginary is not true: You are taught this as children. The imagination, however, brings you into connection with a different kind of truth, or a different framework in which experience can be legitimately perceived. The larger truths of the psyche exist in that dimension.
quotes.translation: 想象的不真实：你从小就被这样教导。然而，想象却让你与另一种真相相连，或与一个可以合法感知经验的框架相连。心灵更广阔的真相存在于那个维度。

quotes.translation: 如果你回忆梦境，可能觉得你处于物质身体中。在这种情况下，你的物质基本假设如此强烈，以至于你无法想象自己即使在梦中也没有物质身体。然而，我们已讨论过实际使用的各种形式。

quotes.text: You may find yourself thinking, "I am no one to begin with," or "The rich get richer and the poor get poorer," or, "The world is against me," or, "Money is wrong. People who have it are not spiritual." You may discover, again, one of numerous beliefs that all lead to the fact that you do not want to have money or are afraid of it. In any case your imagination and your beliefs go hand in hand.
quotes.translation: 你可能会发现自己想着："我本来就是个无名小卒"，或"富者愈富，贫者愈贫"，或"全世界都与我为敌"，或"金钱是罪恶的，有钱人都不灵性"。你可能会再次发现诸多信念，最终都指向你其实不想要钱或害怕钱的事实。无论如何，你的想象力与信念是相辅相成的。

quotes.text: The ramifications of our work will be far greater than either of you usually imagine. It is only natural that the work which will bring so much to others will also serve you abundantly. Otherwise it would be impossible for you to give it as much time as will later be required.
quotes.translation: 我们工作的影响将远比你们通常想象的更为深远。能为他人带来如此多裨益的工作，自然也必将丰盛地回馈于你们，这是理所当然的。否则你们将无法像日后所需的那样投入如此多的时间。

quotes.translation: 若以《三面夏娃》为例——其中实际涉及四个自我——想象每个自我在你们过去、现在与未来的完全独立存在中依次主导，那么你们或许能更接近轮回转世的真实本质。

quotes.text: Also, your actions now can affect the future personality as well as the past one. You must try to stretch your imagination and feel these realities, because the intellect alone cannot comprehend them. Psychological time is your best method for perceiving these actualities.

quotes.text: You perform habitually in certain manners as a result of your beliefs. Now if you willfully change some of those habits then you are also getting the message across. The initiative must come from you, and in the present. In a very real manner of speaking, this means changing your viewpoint, that particular perspective with which you view your past and present and imagine your future.
quotes.translation: 由于信念使然，你们习惯以特定方式行事。若你刻意改变某些习惯，同样也在传递这个讯息。主动权必须来自你，且就在当下。从非常真实的角度来说，这意味着改变你的观点——那个你用以看待过去与现在、想象未来的特定视角。

quotes.text: Now: To some extent or another there are counterparts12 of all realities within your psyche. When you slow down, or quicken your thoughts or your perceptions, you automatically begin to alter your focus, to step aside from your officially recognized existence. This is highly important, for in certain terms you are indeed transcending the time framework that you imagine to be so real.
quotes.translation: 现在：你们心灵中或多或少存在着所有实相的对等物12。当你放慢或加快思想与感知时，便会自动开始转移焦点，从官方认可的存在中抽身。这极为重要，因为从某种层面而言，你确实正在超越那个你想象得如此真实的时空架构。

quotes.translation: 然而这初始经验尚未完成，亦未终结。虽然它无需通过伪装物质世界表达，但其存在的本质远比你们目前所能想象的更为丰沛鲜活。

quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Make sure you do not force the images. Imagine a blank canvas or board, or blank framed picture if you prefer. I will give you an example. This is not a specific suggestion but an example of what I mean.
quotes.translation: 切记不要强迫意象。想象一块空白画布或画板，或者如果你愿意，也可以想象一个空白的带框画。我会给你一个例子。这不是具体的建议，而是我意思的示例。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: I have said more than once that I am no spooky spirit indeed. Nor am I dead. Your egotism, imagining that what you cannot perceive must therefore be either dead or nonexistent, does not even trouble me.
quotes.translation: 我不止一次说过，我确实不是个阴森森的鬼魂。我也没死。你们的自我主义，想象你们无法感知的东西就必然是死的或不存在的，这甚至不会让我烦恼。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json
